Notes: An owner checking underwater with scuba gear at Racine, WI on Lake Michigan touched a bronze propeller and was nearly paralyzed. The cause was a boat in the next slip with a faulty water heater neutral wire. Same location as a fatal incident (ESD #75, May 2006).
Electrical Source Detail: Faulty water heater neutral wire on boat in adjacent slip
Fault Description: Boat in next slip had faulty water heater neutral wire, energizing water around bronze propeller
Research Notes: No independent sources found. Two ESD incidents at same Racine marina within three months (fatal in May, near-miss in August) suggest systemic problems.

Notes: A SCUBA diver felt tingling then moderate shock while approaching a moored sailboat at Collins Bay on Lake Ontario, Kingston, Ontario, Canada. The diver backed away. A battery charger had a short circuit.
Electrical Source Detail: Battery charger on moored sailboat had short circuit
Fault Description: Battery charger short circuit on sailboat energized water
ESDPA Data Issues: Canadian incident (Ontario, Canada); state field uses province code
Research Notes: No independent sources found. Canadian incident. Collins Bay Marina is a well-established facility with approximately 600 slips.

Notes: An adult male was immediately paralyzed at the end of a dock at Lake Arcadia, Edmond, OK. His wife kept his head above water from the dock. A bystander was also shocked. High water had submerged electrical outlets.
Electrical Source Detail: High water submerged electrical outlets at end of dock
Fault Description: Submerged electrical outlets energized water around dock
Research Notes: No independent sources found. Lake Arcadia is a USACE reservoir. High water levels submerging outlets created the electrical hazard.

Notes: A 24-year-old woman attempted to exit the water using a metal ladder at the end of a private dock on Lake of the Ozarks, Missouri. She experienced a paralyzing electric shock which caused her to fall back into the water and drown. Several people had previously reported being shocked by the same ladder, and the dock owner had gone to shut the power off (but apparently did not do so in time). Investigation found the dock power wiring termination was submerged under the dock near the exit point. This is the fourth documented ESD fatality at Lake of the Ozarks, following a ~2004 incident and preceding the 2012 Anderson children deaths and 2015 Colburn death.
Electrical Source Detail: Dock power wiring termination found submerged under the dock near the exit point
Fault Description: Dock power wiring termination was submerged under the dock near the water exit point, energizing the metal ladder
Research Notes: Found via LOA2 (SUSP-2007-052) and LOA6 (LEAD-051). No victim name identified despite searches. Multiple prior shock reports at the same ladder constitute a critical safety failure. Missouri State Highway Patrol Water Division would have the original report. No independent news coverage located. The ESDPA entry for Lake Hamilton AR (FI-2007-001) also carried a July 28, 2007 date but was corrected to August 23, 2008 (Sara Crotts case) — this LOTOZ incident is a separate event on the same ESDPA-listed date.
All Sources Detail:
ESDPA Incident List (Rev. 5/9/16) (other) http://www.electricshockdrowning.org/uploads/4/8/5/6/48564375/esd_incident_list_-_updated_5.9.16.pdf

Notes: Ryan O'Gorman, 22, a recent Cornell University graduate, entered a private farm pond in Eden, NY to rescue a dog. He was thrown back and suffered cardiac arrest (no heartbeat for 20 minutes). His father, Dr. O'Gorman (a physician), performed CPR along with Lt. John McCarthy (Eden police officer/volunteer firefighter). The dog was killed by the electrical shock. A submersible irrigation pump with deteriorated wiring (installed before 1994) was the cause. Ryan walked out of Mercy Hospital four days later.
Electrical Source Detail: Submersible irrigation pump with deteriorated wire sheathing, not grounded; installed prior to 1994
Fault Description: Submersible irrigation pump had deteriorated wire sheathing and was not grounded
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date states August 28; day of week analysis suggests possible August 27 (Monday)
Research Notes: Victim identified through independent sources. Property owner Christina Abt was not charged; pump installation predated her ownership. Series of improbable coincidences (physician father nearby, nurse friend, helicopter returning from another call) saved his life.

Notes: A boat owner cleaning a prop shaft in the water at Franklin Lock Campground on the Caloosahatchee River, FL was shocked. His teeth clenched involuntarily and he saw blue sparks. He exited the water. The breaker had tripped.
Electrical Source Detail: Shore power fault; breaker tripped
Fault Description: Electrical fault caused arcing (blue sparks visible); breaker tripped
Research Notes: No independent sources found. Teeth clenching and visible blue sparks indicate significant electrical exposure. Self-rescue prevented potential drowning.

Facility Name: Ocean Isle Beach Marina, 2000 Sommerset Rd SW
Notes: A construction worker employed by Coastal Carolina Construction & Development, Inc. was electrocuted while performing repair work on a floating dock at Ocean Isle Beach Marina. He was using an electric drill with his feet in the water, plugged into a marina receptacle that lacked GFCI protection. He was pronounced dead at Brunswick Community Hospital. OSHA issued three serious violations totaling $3,360 in penalties, including citation for 29 CFR 1926.404(B)(1)(ii) — GFCI requirement for electrical equipment in wet locations.
Electrical Source Detail: Electric drill plugged into marina receptacle lacking GFCI protection; worker had feet in water
Fault Description: Marina receptacle lacked GFCI protection; worker using electric drill with feet in water on floating dock was electrocuted
Research Notes: Occupational fatality at a marina — ESD-adjacent. Victim was a construction worker, not recreational. OSHA Accident Summary Nr 200357044, Inspection Nr 311618052. Employer: Coastal Carolina Construction & Development, Inc., Supply NC. Case closed June 25, 2015.

Notes: Sara Crotts, 14, of Sherwood, Arkansas, died from electrocution with drowning at Lake Hamilton Resort on Saturday, August 23, 2008. She was swimming with Tamara Keele, 40, and her son Devin Keele, 6, both of Sherwood, at approximately 8 p.m. The 7-year-old boy (per ESDPA, 6 per news) was being shocked and Crotts attempted to help him. 103 VAC was measured on the ladder. An unbonded metal pole welded to the dock with a halogen light was involved in the electrical fault.
All Victims: Sara Crotts (14, F); Tamara Keele (40, F); Devin Keele (6, M)
Electrical Source Detail: 103 VAC measured on ladder; unbonded metal pole welded to dock with halogen light
Fault Description: Metal ladder energized at 103 VAC; unbonded metal pole welded to dock with halogen light
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 28, 2007; independent sources confirm August 23, 2008; ESDPA lists 7-year-old boy; independent sources say 6-year-old boy (Devin Keele); Date error of over 1 year; no independent sources found for any July 2007 Lake Hamilton ESD incident
Research Notes: ESDPA date of July 28, 2007 is wrong by over a year. No 2007 Lake Hamilton ESD incident found in any independent source. The similarities (14-year-old girl, young boy, Lake Hamilton Resort, ladder, electrical shock) strongly suggest this is the same incident as the well-documented August 23, 2008 Crotts case. Arkansas Democrat-Gazette (Aug 31, 2008) and KAIT8 confirm the 2008 date. The age discrepancy (7 vs 6) is minor.

Facility Name: Neighbor's dock at 3830 N. Shore Drive
Notes: Nickolas Greiner, 30, from Ankeny, jumped into Clear Lake at his parents' home (3830 N. Shore Drive) to rescue a dog struggling underwater near a neighbor's dock hoist. A family member recognized the electrical hazard and disconnected the dock power. Greiner was treated at Mercy Medical Center-North Iowa in Mason City with burns and bruising. The dog died.
Electrical Source Detail: Neighbor's dock hoist (boat lift) energizing water
Fault Description: Dock hoist electrical fault; DNR noted dock electrical service did not meet federal standards
Research Notes: Fully confirmed by Globe Gazette article from day after incident. DNR Conservation Officer Ben Bergman commented on dock electrical standards. Neighbor's dock hoist was the source.

Notes: Michael Andrew Cunningham, 15, of Bridgeport, WV, a sophomore at Bridgeport High School, was electrocuted at Stonewall Jackson Lake Marina on Saturday, May 29, 2010. He was swimming with three other youths behind a docked boat. Cunningham had exited the water and was standing on the back of the boat when he touched the swim ladder and received an electric shock, falling into the water and disappearing. Body recovered at 34-foot depth nearly 5 hours later. Autopsy showed no water in his lungs — death was from electrocution, not drowning. Current from the dock had leaked into the water around the houseboat. Another youth was also shocked but survived. His mother Amy Cunningham became a major ESD safety advocate.
Legal Outcome: Michael Cunningham Act (West Virginia, signed May 29, 2013) — requires GFCI outlets at marina docks, emergency shut-off, recurring statewide marina safety inspections, increased warning signage
Electrical Source Detail: Current from dock leaked into water around houseboat; ESDPA describes reversed hot/ground in shore cord
Fault Description: Dock electrical current leaking into water around houseboat; reversed hot/ground in shore cord (per ESDPA, not independently confirmed)
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as August 23, 2008; correct date is May 29, 2010 per obituary, news, and legislation signing; ESDPA says 'fell back in and drowned' but autopsy found no water in lungs — death was electrocution, not drowning; Date error of nearly 2 years
Research Notes: ESDPA date of Aug 23, 2008 is wrong by nearly 2 years. The 3rd anniversary signing of the Michael Cunningham Act on May 29, 2013 independently confirms the death date as May 29, 2010. Parents: Marty Knudsen [sic - should be Cunningham] and Tina Tokar per one source, but WV Public Broadcasting names mother as Amy Cunningham. Active in marching band and concert band. The marina later discontinued all electrical service to boat slips rather than pay for $600,000+ safety upgrades required by the Act.

The Doan Law Firm (news) https://accidents.thedoanlawfirm.com/blog/2010/july/electric-shock-likely-cause-of-west-virginia-dro/ [2010-07-06]

Burnside Funeral Home / TributeArchive (obituary) https://www.tributearchive.com/obituaries/11553974/Michael-Cunningham/wall [2010]
The Times West Virginian / Yahoo News (news) https://www.yahoo.com/news/bridgeports-amy-cunningham-honored-woman-145000049.html [2023-11]

Facility Name: Private covered dock with boat lift, approximately 200 feet from shore
Notes: A boy was killed swimming near a covered dock with a boat lift at Lake Waccamaw, North Carolina, on May 29, 2010. A grandfather had taken children to play under the dock in 3 feet of water. He raised the metal boat lift rails about 2 feet above water to create swimming space. One child reported feeling a 'tingle' in his legs before the fatal incident. The grandfather saw a child floating face down and another child raised the boy's head, finding him unconscious. CPR was unsuccessful. Investigation found the underground feeder cable's equipment ground conductor had been severed at a junction box in the house crawl space, and a water-filled junction box beneath the dock created a current path. The dock was approximately 200 feet from shore with amenities including ceiling fans, picnic table, and barbecue grill. The installation was unpermitted, done by an unlicensed contractor, and lacked required GFCI protection.
Electrical Source Detail: Severed equipment ground conductor in house crawl space; water-filled junction box beneath dock creating conductive path between live wire and water
Fault Description: Open ground in underground feeder cable at junction box in house crawl space; water-filled plastic junction box beneath dock; missing GFCI protection; unpermitted installation by unlicensed contractor
Research Notes: EC&M technical article provides detailed analysis. Children swimming ranged from 8 to 13 years old but the victim is not identified by name. May 29, 2010 was a Saturday. This is the second documented ESD fatality at Lake Waccamaw (the first was in June 2004, also at a dock with a boat lift).

Notes: A 16-year-old boy fell back into the water while climbing a dock ladder at Lewis Smith Lake, Alabama, in June 2010. Electrocution was suspected as the cause of death. No further details are available.
Fault Description: Electrocution suspected; boy fell back into water while climbing dock ladder
ESDPA Data Issues: Only month-level date precision (June 2010); Location listed as Birmingham AL but Smith Lake spans Cullman, Walker, Winston, and Blount counties, none of which are in Birmingham
Research Notes: No independent sources found despite extensive searching. Smith Lake had a well-documented 2016 ESD death (Carmen Johnson, 15) but no 2010 incident could be located in news archives, obituaries, or official records. The victim's name, specific date, and exact location on the lake are unknown. Smith Lake spans multiple counties (Cullman, Walker, Winston, Blount), complicating searches.

Notes: A 54-year-old man died jumping into a swimming pool in York, South Carolina, to rescue his granddaughter who was being shocked by the pool's railing. The granddaughter survived the incident.
Electrical Source Detail: Pool railing was energized with electrical current
Fault Description: Pool railing was electrified; specific fault not documented
ESDPA Data Issues: Date may be July 10, 2010 rather than June 21, 2010; one secondary reference cites July 10; June 21 was a Monday; July 10 was a Saturday (more typical for pool incident)
Research Notes: No independent sources found. Date may be June 21, 2010 or July 10, 2010 (secondary references conflict). Local newspaper (The Herald) archives not accessible online for 2010. This is a pool electrocution rather than a dock/marina ESD incident.

Notes: A 15-year-old girl and her 8-year-old sister were shocked near a boat dock at Lake Travis, TX. The older sister was unresponsive and in critical condition in the hospital. Shoddy boat lift wiring was identified as the cause. Both girls survived.
Electrical Source Detail: Shoddy boat lift wiring
Fault Description: Defective boat lift wiring energized water near dock
Research Notes: No independent sources found despite critical hospitalization of minor victim. Privacy protections for juvenile victims may have limited press coverage.

Notes: Three females (ages 12, 20, and 30) were shocked near a docked boat at International Harbor, Friendsville, TN. One was not breathing when EMS arrived and was revived. A 63-year-old woman was also shocked. All four victims were hospitalized but all survived.
All Victims: Unknown (12, F); Unknown (20, F); Unknown (30, F); Unknown (63, F)
Electrical Source Detail: Electrical fault near docked boat at marina
Research Notes: No independent sources found despite one victim requiring resuscitation. Fort Loudoun Lake is a TVA reservoir. Holiday weekend (July 3) may have affected coverage.

Notes: Beth Waite was swimming behind a houseboat at Green River Lake when she was shocked, arched back, and began sinking. Her boyfriend John Childress felt shocks from the boat ramp but avoided entering the water. Houseboat owner Bobby Gullett turned off the power and jumped in to rescue Beth, but was also severely shocked by voltage still present in the water. Both Beth and Bobby received emergency medical care. All survived.
All Victims: Beth Waite (F); Bobby Gullett (M); John Childress (M)
Electrical Source Detail: Houseboat electrical system energizing water; voltage persisted even after power was turned off
Fault Description: Houseboat electrical fault; Bobby Gullett was shocked even after power was turned off
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date was August 8, 2010; correct date is July 10, 2010
Research Notes: ESDPA date corrected from August 8 to July 10, 2010 per Poynter and HuffPost articles. Critical detail: voltage persisted in water after power was turned off.

Notes: Zachary Joshua 'Zach' Crays, 13, of Lafayette IN, was electrocuted while swimming with friends near a dock at Angel Shores Resort on Lake Freeman. A water-soaked junction box energized the dock metal. He felt a tingle, tried to exit, and fell back into the water. He died at 10:55 AM at White County Memorial Hospital. Classic electric shock drowning case.
Legal Outcome: Jury awarded $3M in wrongful death case. Fault: Bryan Wagner 95%, Angel Shores Mobile Home Park 5%.
Electrical Source Detail: Water-soaked electrical junction box on dock conducted current through dock metal
Fault Description: Water-soaked junction box energized dock metal; faulty wiring at property leased by Bryan and Donna Wagner
ESDPA Entry Numbers: FI-XXXX-001 (project file, no date in ESDPA)
ESDPA Data Issues: No date provided in ESDPA document; confirmed date is July 25, 2010
Research Notes: ESDPA had no date; now confirmed as July 25, 2010. Court case (78 N.E.3d 718) provides extensive details. LOA8 obituary mining research (March 2026) confirmed full name Zachary Joshua Crays, son of John W. and Laura Megan McGow Crays of Lafayette IN. Legacy.com obituary PID 144325982 confirms death at 10:55 AM at White County Memorial Hospital. Coroner ruled cause as electrocution caused by faulty electric wiring. Carroll County REMC confirmed water-soaked junction box.
All Sources Detail:

Legacy.com (obituary) https://www.legacy.com/obituaries/name/zachary-crays-obituary?pid=144325982 [2010-07-26]

Notes: A father and two 13-year-old sons were shocked at Lake Champlain, Vermont. All three survived. No further details available about electrical source, specific location, or circumstances.
Research Notes: Confirmed by HuffPost article. Minimal details available beyond date, location, victims, and outcome.

Body of Water: Small lake at Perrine Wayside Dog Park
Facility Name: Perrine Wayside Dog Park (Village Dog Park), 16425 S. Dixie Highway
Notes: A dog (Ruger, 6-month-old Labrador retriever belonging to Victor Garcia) died in the lake at Perrine Wayside Dog Park, Palmetto Bay FL. A man was stopped by shocks when attempting to enter the water. The fountain was still electrifying the water after being turned off. The fountain, pump, and wiring were completely removed and not replaced. Park was closed for most of September 2010.
Electrical Source Detail: Fountain pump and wiring in park lake energizing water; remained energized even after being turned off
Fault Description: Fountain was still electrifying the water after being turned off; fountain, pump, and wiring completely removed
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date was September 5, 2010; correct date is September 1, 2010
Research Notes: ESDPA date corrected from September 5 to September 1, 2010 per Palmetto Bay Community News. Park opened November 2007; fountain permanently removed after incident.

Notes: A 12-year-old girl grabbed an electrical wire at Hartwell Lake, Anderson, SC and went into cardiac arrest. She was resuscitated by her father and airlifted to the hospital. A grounding problem on the dock was found. She survived.
Electrical Source Detail: Grounding problem on dock; girl grabbed electrical wire
Fault Description: Grounding problem on dock
ESDPA Data Issues: Date may be uncertain; originally listed same date as Perrine Wayside incident which was corrected
Research Notes: No independent sources found. Cardiac arrest and airlift indicate extremely serious near-fatal incident. Father's CPR saved her life.

Notes: A dog died in a retention pond fountain in Houston, TX. A man felt strong current at ankle depth and retreated. Power was secured.
Electrical Source Detail: Faulty retention pond fountain pump/wiring
Fault Description: Fountain electrified retention pond water
Research Notes: No detailed independent sources found. A Pond Boss Forum reference suggests incident occurred and may have had some local news coverage that is no longer accessible online.

Notes: Brian Eugene Harwick, 50, of Lake City, Minnesota, was electrocuted on Saturday, May 28, 2011, at a family home at 1400 Central Point on the north side of Lake City. His dog ran into the water and swam over a boat lift that had an electrical track running underwater to the shore. The dog received a shock and began to sink. Another person jumped in first to rescue the dog but was also shocked and managed to exit the water. Harwick then jumped in, was shocked and rendered unconscious, and went underwater. Friends and relatives pulled him from the water and attempted CPR, but he died at Mayo Clinic Health System's hospital. Harwick worked at the Kemps ice cream plant. His brother Tom had died in February and his mother in March of the same year.
Electrical Source Detail: Boat lift had an electrical track running underwater to the shore that was energized
Fault Description: Boat lift electrical track running underwater to shore was energized
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 26, 2010; correct date is May 28, 2011 per Find a Grave, forum, and family obituaries; Date error of nearly 1 year
Research Notes: ESDPA date of July 26, 2010 is wrong by nearly a year. Find a Grave confirms: born June 1, 1960 Rochester MN, died May 28, 2011 Lake City, Wabasha County MN. Buried at Oakwood Cemetery, Rochester. Brother Thomas 'Burger' Harwick died Feb 25, 2011 (obituary lists Brian as surviving brother). Mother Helen Marie Harwick died March 2011. Partner: Barb Sperry.
All Sources Detail:
In-Depth Outdoors Forum (quoting Goodhue County Sheriff's Office) (other) https://www.in-depthoutdoors.com/community/forums/topic/ftlgeneral_1016799/ [2011-06]
Find a Grave (memorial) https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/217661827/brian-eugene-harwick
Ranfranz and Vine Funeral Homes (brother Thomas's obituary) (obituary) https://www.ranfranzandvinefh.com/obituaries/thomas-burger-leslie-harwick [2011-02]

Notes: A woman was overcome by electrical current in the water via a boat ladder at Lou Wendell Marine on the Kanawha River, Charleston, WV. She was submerged for 90 seconds before being rescued. A grounded neutral in the pedestal was identified as the cause.
Electrical Source Detail: Grounded neutral in pedestal
Fault Description: Grounded neutral in shore power pedestal energized water via boat ladder
Research Notes: No independent sources found. 90-second submersion is extremely dangerous — survival was fortunate. Lou Wendell Marine confirmed to exist on the Kanawha River near St. Albans/Tornado area.

Facility Name: Cornfield with center pivot irrigation system
Notes: Hannah Kendall and Jade Garza, both 14, of Sterling, Illinois, were electrocuted on Monday, July 25, 2011, while detasseling corn in a field near Tampico. The girls were best friends working with a crew of approximately 70 others. They stepped into a pool of standing water in the field that had been electrified by a defective meter on the center pivot irrigation system. Lightning had reportedly struck the meter box, energizing the system. Jade received the initial shock, and Hannah was killed trying to save her. Eight other workers were injured, one critically. OSHA investigated but issued no citations. Both funerals, held July 29, opened with the same song: 'If I Die Young.' The girls would have been freshmen at Sterling High School that fall.
All Victims: Hannah Kendall (14, F); Jade Garza (14, F)
Legal Outcome: OSHA investigated; no citations issued
Electrical Source Detail: Lightning struck meter box of center pivot irrigation system, energizing electrical components and causing current to flow into standing water in field
Fault Description: Lightning damaged meter box associated with center pivot irrigator; electrical system components became energized, releasing current into standing water
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA year listed as 2012; correct year is 2011 per all independent sources; ESDPA says '14yo boy and girl'; both victims were girls (Hannah Kendall and Jade Garza)
Research Notes: ESDPA year is wrong (2012 instead of 2011). ESDPA says 'boy and girl' but both victims were girls. OSHA found no workplace safety violations. Workers included teens as young as 13. This is an agricultural/irrigation electrocution, not a dock/marina ESD incident.

Notes: A 25-year-old woman was electrocuted at Lake Sinclair, Putnam County, Georgia, on August 15, 2011. She reached from the water and touched either a box or bare wire between two docked houseboats. The dock was being worked on at the time. An 8-year-old girl swimming in the area received a shock but survived. A second woman was shocked when she pulled the 25-year-old away from the dock and attempted to hold her head above water.
Electrical Source Detail: Box or bare wire between two docked houseboats on dock being worked on
Fault Description: Electrical hazard between two docked houseboats; dock was under construction/repair
Research Notes: No independent sources found despite extensive searching. August 15, 2011 was a Monday, which is less typical for recreational swimming. The dock work may have been professional (weekday) rather than weekend DIY. The involvement of active construction/repair raises questions about whether lockout/tagout and safety protocols were followed.

Notes: Michael Scott Knudsen, 18, of Mancelona, MI, was electrocuted at the Duncan L. Clinch Marina on August 15, 2011. Knudsen and friend Zachary Kott-Millard jumped from F-Dock into West Grand Traverse Bay at approximately 7:30 p.m. Knudsen immediately struggled, bleeding from his eyes, ears, and nose due to electrical shock. Friends who tried to grab his arms were also shocked. Marina employees cut power, and two firefighters found Knudsen unresponsive beneath the dock. Autopsy determined death from 'a combination of electrical shock and drowning.' Investigation found a short circuit in a 220-volt line with corroded insulation in metal junction boxes (the design specified fiberglass), a failed circuit breaker, and corroded equipment ground materials. Swimming was prohibited at the marina. Prior users had reported feeling electrical current near the dock.
Legal Outcome: $50 million lawsuit filed by Geoffrey Fieger against City of Traverse City, SmithGroup JJR LLC, Rhoades Engineering Corp., and others; City dismissed on governmental immunity; family later sued Fieger for malpractice; no compensation received
Electrical Source Detail: Short circuit in 220-volt line with corroded/worn insulation in metal junction box (design specified fiberglass boxes); circuit breaker failed to trip; corroded, broken, and overheated equipment ground materials
Fault Description: Short circuit in 220V line; metal junction boxes installed instead of specified fiberglass; corroded equipment ground; circuit breaker failure
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as May 28, 2011; correct date is August 15, 2011 per obituary, AP, and all news sources
Research Notes: ESDPA date of May 28, 2011 is wrong; correct date is August 15, 2011 per unanimous sources. Born July 29, 1993. Parents: Marty Knudsen and Tina Tokar. The $50M Fieger lawsuit was dismissed on governmental immunity. Family sued Fieger for malpractice; Fieger filed frivolous countersuit and was sanctioned $130,000+. Family received no compensation. The marina renovation was completed in 2003 by SmithGroup JJR and Rhoades Engineering.

Notes: During Hurricane Irene, a dog was electrocuted in floodwater near Shady Beach, Norwalk, CT. Two women felt shocks. Witnesses reported the 'water was boiling' from electrical current. Hurricane Irene caused widespread flooding and 767,000 power outages across Connecticut.
Electrical Source Detail: Hurricane Irene floodwater energized by downed power lines or submerged electrical equipment
Fault Description: Storm flooding energized water; witnesses reported 'water was boiling'
Research Notes: No independent sources found for this specific incident, though Hurricane Irene's impact on Norwalk is well-documented. The incident was likely overshadowed by the broader hurricane disaster response.

Notes: Three women were shocked while swimming near a dock at Lake of the Ozarks, MO on Memorial Day weekend. Someone turned off the power. One woman needed CPR. All survived. No GFCI was present and the ground wire was inadequate.
Electrical Source Detail: No GFCI present; inadequate ground wire
Fault Description: No GFCI protection; inadequate ground wire on dock
Research Notes: No detailed independent sources found. A personal injury law blog mentions Memorial Day 2012 'swimmer electrocution' at Lake of the Ozarks with no fatalities, potentially corroborating. Occurred five weeks before the fatal July 4, 2012 incident.

Facility Name: Orange Lake Resort, miniature golf course
Notes: 11-year-old Ashton Jojo from Latham, NY, was electrocuted while playing miniature golf at Orange Lake Resort. She stepped into a 2-foot-deep pond to retrieve her golf ball. A bystander, Christopher Burges, attempted rescue but was also shocked. Cause was a faulty pump connected to the wrong circuit breaker with missing GFCI.
Legal Outcome: Family filed $27M lawsuit; settled for $10M within nine months.
Electrical Source Detail: Faulty pump connected to wrong circuit breaker; missing GFCI protection on pond fountain
Fault Description: Decorative fountain pump was connected to wrong circuit breaker; non-GFI breakers employed; pump improperly replaced without permits
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as May 5, 2012; correct date is June 27, 2012; Location listed as Celebration FL; actually Orange Lake Resort near Kissimmee
Research Notes: ESDPA date of May 5, 2012 is incorrect; correct date is June 27, 2012. She was on vacation with her family and had celebrated her birthday June 22.

Facility Name: Private dock, Gravois Arm, 6.5 Mile Marker
Notes: Alexandra Anderson (13) and her brother Brayden Anderson (8), both of Ashland MO, were electrocuted while swimming near the family's vacation home dock at the 6.5 mile marker of the Gravois Arm, Lake of the Ozarks. A chafed hot wire had shorted to the unbonded dock framing, sending stray current into the water. Several adults pulled the children out and performed CPR. Both were pronounced dead at Lake Regional Hospital, Osage Beach. Mother Angela Anderson became a leading national ESD safety advocate and testified before Missouri lawmakers for dock safety legislation (Rep. Jake Hummel bill).
All Victims: Alexandra Anderson (13, F); Brayden Anderson (8, M)
Legal Outcome: Anderson v. Ameren Missouri — Missouri Supreme Court ruled 5-2 in favor of Ameren (June 2015), citing Recreational Use Act. Angela Anderson's wrongful death claim dismissed despite confirmed electrical cause.
Electrical Source Detail: Chafed hot wire shorted to unbonded dock framing
Fault Description: Improperly grounded electrical circuit to the dock; no ground-fault protection
Research Notes: Part of the LOTOZ cluster — deadliest ESD location in the U.S. with 6 confirmed deaths across 5 incidents (2004-2015). Three days later (July 7, 2012), Jennifer Lankford died at a separate dock at 4 Mile Marker — see ESD-2012-07-07-1.
All Sources Detail:
Lake Expo (news) https://www.lakeexpo.com/news/lake_news/two-children-die-from-electrocution-on-gravois-arm/article_086f71be-c61d-11e1-840d-0019bb2963f4.html [2012-07-04]
Columbia Missourian (news) https://www.columbiamissourian.com/news/local/childrens-deaths-start-life-of-awareness-raising-for-ashland-woman/article_db313754-d4c4-52fc-a8cb-3499de444182.html [2017]
St. Louis Public Radio (news) https://www.stlpr.org/government-politics-issues/2015-06-16/is-ameren-responsible-for-electrocutions-on-lake-of-the-ozarks-missouri-supreme-court-says-no [2015-06-16]
ABC News (video) https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Dst7CW2PNxc [2012-07]
FindLaw (court) https://caselaw.findlaw.com/mo-court-of-appeals/1669365.html

Notes: Two boys, friends and classmates at Cornerstone Academy in Morristown TN, died after being electrocuted while swimming at German Creek Marina on Cherokee Lake. Noah died July 4; Nate died July 5 on life support. Nate's 8-year-old sister was also shocked and hospitalized overnight. Frayed wiring on a houseboat combined with lack of GFI protection at the marina. Incident led to Tennessee's Noah Dean and Nate Act (2014).
All Victims: Noah Winstead (10, M); Nate Lynam (11, M); Unknown Lynam (8, F); Unknown Lynam (M)
Legal Outcome: Parents filed $3M lawsuits against marina owner James Figuerado. Tennessee passed Noah Dean and Nate Act (2014) requiring GFCI at marinas.
Electrical Source Detail: Frayed/faulty wiring aboard a houseboat; marina lacked ground fault interrupters
Fault Description: Houseboat with faulty wiring coupled with marina's lack of GFI protection energized water near metal ladder
ESDPA Data Issues: ESD section date listed as June 27, 2012; correct date is July 4, 2012; Victims unnamed in ESDPA list
Research Notes: ESDPA date of June 27 is incorrect; all sources confirm July 4, 2012. Both boys were from Morristown, TN.

Facility Name: Private dock, Dry Branch Cove, 5-mile marker
Notes: Jennifer Lankford, 26, of Hazelwood, Missouri, was electrocuted on Saturday, July 7, 2012, at approximately 7:45 p.m. at Dry Branch Cove, Morgan County, at the 5-mile marker of Lake of the Ozarks. Lankford was swimming with two children (her stepbrothers, ages 11 and 13) when she touched a dock with faulty wiring and was electrocuted. The children survived. Missouri Highway Patrol Sgt. Paul Reinsch investigated. This was the second fatal electrocution at Lake of the Ozarks that week — three days earlier, on July 4, siblings Alexandra Anderson (13) and Brayden Anderson (8) had been killed at a different dock.
Electrical Source Detail: Private dock with faulty wiring at Dry Branch Cove
Fault Description: Faulty dock wiring energized dock structure
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 4, 2012; correct date is July 7, 2012 per Columbia Missourian, Lake Expo, and other sources; ESDPA confusion likely arose because a separate incident (Anderson children) occurred on July 4 at the same lake
Research Notes: ESDPA date of July 4 is wrong by 3 days; confusion likely arose from the Anderson children incident that actually occurred on July 4 at the same lake. The Columbia Missourian article (published July 8) reports the incident on Saturday July 7. The cluster of three ESD deaths in one week at Lake of the Ozarks prompted significant regulatory attention.

Lake Expo (news) https://www.lakeexpo.com/news/lake_news/update-woman-dies-of-dock-electrocution-in-dry-branch-cove/article_d1e06d38-c978-11e1-b37c-0019bb2963f4.html [2012-07-08]
St. Louis Post-Dispatch (news) https://www.stltoday.com/news/local/metro/docks-with-bad-wiring-prove-deadly-at-lake-of-the-ozarks/article_876aa21d-60c0-5b07-85d2-551fa76c6a3b.html [2012-07]
FOX 2 Now (St. Louis) (news) https://fox2now.com/news/another-swimmer-electrocuted-at-lake-of-the-ozarks/ [2012-07]

Notes: Two children and an adult near a dock under electrical repairs at Lake of the Ozarks, MO all felt electric shock. Power was turned off and everyone exited safely. Occurred 19 days after fatal Anderson children incident (July 4) and 16 days after Jennifer Lankford fatality (July 7).
Electrical Source Detail: Dock under electrical repairs; power turned off after shock detected
Fault Description: Electrical hazard during dock repair work
Research Notes: No independent sources found. Electrical repairs likely initiated in response to July 2012 fatalities. KSMU radio reported on dock safety on July 24, 2012, one day after this incident.

Notes: Robert Stoen, 22, of Owatonna, MN, was electrocuted when an extension cord fell into the water from a boat lift at a private dock on Pokegama Lake (Hill Top Trail, Cohasset, MN). Eric Hancock (35) and Cheryl Hancock (57), also of Owatonna, were shocked and hospitalized in serious but stable condition. The Itasca County Sheriff's Office responded. This occurred less than a month after the July 4, 2012 Cherokee Lake (TN) ESD deaths and was part of a 2012 summer cluster of ESD fatalities.
All Victims: Robert Stoen (22, M); Eric Hancock (35, M); Cheryl Hancock (57, F)
Electrical Source Detail: Extension cord fell into water from boat lift at private dock
Fault Description: Extension cord fell from boat lift structure into water, energizing surrounding area
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 7, 2012; correct date is July 25, 2012 per CBS Minnesota and AuGres Maritime Museum; ESDPA did not include victim names; CBS Minnesota identifies Robert Stoen (22, Owatonna MN)
Research Notes: ESDPA date wrong by 18 days (July 7 vs July 25). LOA11 YouTube research and CBS Minnesota confirmed victim identities and correct date. AuGres Maritime Museum list #14 corroborates. Filename retains ESDPA date (07-07) for cross-reference integrity; actual date is 07-25.

Notes: A woman and her husband both confirmed shock while reaching for a dock ladder at Gravois Arm on Lake of the Ozarks, MO. They swam away to a neighbor's dock and survived. Same area as July 4, 2012 fatal incident (Anderson children).
Electrical Source Detail: Dock ladder energized
Fault Description: Dock ladder energized; couple swam away to neighbor's dock
Research Notes: No independent sources found. Occurred one month after double-fatality at same location (Gravois Arm). Highly plausible given documented pattern of ESD incidents at Lake of the Ozarks in summer 2012.

Notes: Bianca Cruz (15) and her sister Tiffany Cruz (23) were swimming in their backyard pool in Garden Grove, CA around noon when electrical current ran through the water. Bianca was at the deep end and became unresponsive, requiring CPR by Garden Grove police officers Claudia Alarcon and Edgar Valencia. Their father Julio Cruz was shocked pulling Bianca from the pool. Both sisters were hospitalized at UCI Medical Center. Bianca was expected to make a full recovery.
All Victims: Bianca Cruz (15, F); Tiffany Cruz (23, F); Julio Cruz (M)
Electrical Source Detail: Pool electrical fault; pool had underwater lighting and recent electrical work
Fault Description: Exact cause undetermined; pool had underwater light and recent work
Research Notes: Verified by ABC7 and NBC Los Angeles coverage. Pool had underwater lighting and recent work done. Exact electrical cause was not determined at time of reporting.
All Sources Detail:
ABC7 / KABC-TV Los Angeles (news) https://abc7.com/archive/8772430/ [2012-08-14]
NBC Los Angeles / KNBC-TV (news) https://www.nbclosangeles.com/news/local/Family-Cruz-Pool-Shock-Garden-Grove-Girl-Who-Cheated-Death-Speaks-Out-166192616.html [2012-08]

Facility Name: Magnolia Boulevard at Ben Avenue, Valley Village
Notes: Two Good Samaritan women were electrocuted at approximately 8:25 p.m. on Wednesday, August 22, 2012, in Valley Village (Los Angeles). Driver Arman Samsonian lost control of his SUV at high speed, crashed into a fire hydrant and light pole at the intersection of Magnolia Boulevard and Ben Avenue. Water from the fire hydrant contacted exposed electrical lines, creating an electrified puddle estimated at 4,800 volts. Irma Yolanda Zamora, 40, of Burbank, and Stacey Lee Schreiber, 39, of Valley Village, witnessed the crash and rushed to help the driver. They were electrocuted by the energized water. Approximately eight other people, including an LAPD officer, were also shocked while attempting to help. LAFD firefighters received medals of valor for their rescue efforts.
All Victims: Irma Zamora (40, F); Stacey Schreiber (39, F)
Legal Outcome: Driver Arman Samsonian pleaded no contest to vehicular manslaughter; sentenced to 3 years formal probation and 70 days community labor. Families filed wrongful death lawsuits against City of Los Angeles and DWP.
Electrical Source Detail: Car crash knocked down fire hydrant and light pole; water from hydrant contacted exposed electrical lines at 4,800 volts
Fault Description: Car crash into fire hydrant and light pole; water from fire hydrant contacted exposed electrical lines
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 25, 2012; correct date is August 22, 2012 per LAPD press release, ABC News, and all coverage
Research Notes: ESDPA date of July 25, 2012 is off by approximately 28 days. This is an overhead power line/car crash incident, not a classic dock/marina ESD case. Both dates (July 25 and August 22) were Wednesdays. LAPD Valley Traffic Division investigated.

Facility Name: Irrigation canal approximately 3 miles NW of Blackfoot
Notes: Three people were electrocuted in an irrigation canal approximately 3 miles northwest of Blackfoot, Idaho, on Thursday, August 30, 2012, at approximately 9:30 p.m. Jacquelyn R. Paulson, 31, an Idaho State University assistant track and field coach and two-time NCAA All-American in the heptathlon, went missing while searching for the family dog. The dog had entered the electrified water and was shocked. Michael Lance Hicks, 41, and Preston Keith Tarpley, 49, both of Blackfoot, entered the water to rescue Paulson and were also electrocuted. The cause was a broken ground wire between the transformer pole and the service pole, which energized the ground wire between the service pole and the irrigation pump, electrifying the canal. Circuit breakers did not trip.
All Victims: Jacquelyn Paulson (31, F); Michael Hicks (41, M); Preston Tarpley (49, M)
Electrical Source Detail: Broken ground wire between transformer pole and service pole energized the ground wire between service pole and irrigation pump, electrifying the canal water
Fault Description: Broken ground wire between transformer pole and service pole; irrigation pump shorted out; circuit breakers did not trip
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as August 22, 2012; correct date is August 30, 2012 per ESPN, Idaho State Journal, AP
Research Notes: ESDPA date of Aug 22 is off by 8 days; correct date is Aug 30, 2012. Paulson competed in the 2004 and 2008 Olympic Trials (8th in 2008 heptathlon). She was going into her 9th season as ISU assistant coach. The dog also died. Bingham County Sheriff's Office investigated. This is an irrigation/agricultural electrocution, not a classic dock/marina ESD incident.

Notes: William 'Will' Nolan, 39, a professor of film, literary, and cultural studies in the Honors College at the University of Alabama, was electrocuted at Tims Ford Lake on September 27, 2012. He was visiting family in Winchester, Tennessee, and went for a swim around 7 p.m. after returning from a jog. After about an hour, his mother-in-law walked to the dock and found his clothing. Authorities found him under the dock. Investigation revealed the house had been struck by lightning in the recent past, causing faulty wiring from the house to the dock that energized the area with electricity. Born May 27, 1973, in West Chelmsford, Massachusetts. He was an Eagle Scout and left behind a wife and newborn son (born August 2012).
Electrical Source Detail: Lightning had struck the house, damaging wiring from house to dock, causing area around dock to be live with electricity
Fault Description: Lightning strike damaged wiring from house to dock, energizing the water around the dock
Research Notes: ESDPA date is correct (September 2012 per file, confirmed as September 27 by obituary and news). Parents: Bill and Marsha Nolan of West Chelmsford, MA. Memorial fund established: Will Nolan Memorial Fund for Film Studies at UA Honors College.

Facility Name: South Georgia Technical College, decorative fountain
Notes: Adriana Rhine, 18, a student at South Georgia Technical College, was electrocuted on September 27, 2012, after entering a decorative fountain on campus to retrieve her young son's ball. When bystanders attempted to help her, they also received electrical shocks, preventing immediate rescue. She was transported to Phoebe Sumter Medical Center where she was pronounced dead. Post-incident inspection found 17 electrical code violations that made the fountain a hazard.
Legal Outcome: $1.4 million settlement (maximum under Georgia state law): $1 million wrongful death + $400,000 pain and suffering; entire amount placed in trust for Rhine's son ZiQuan Bennett (4)
Electrical Source Detail: Decorative fountain on college campus with 17 electrical code violations
Fault Description: Fountain had 17 electrical code violations creating electrocution hazard
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as August 30, 2012; WALB news report published September 27, 2012 describes incident as just occurring; ESDPA lists victim age as 19; confirmed age is 18 per multiple independent sources
Research Notes: ESDPA date of Aug 30, 2012 appears to be incorrect; WALB news report published Sep 27, 2012 describes the incident as having just occurred. Age is 18 per all independent sources, not 19. Her son ZiQuan Bennett was approximately 4 years old. The $1.4M settlement (2014) was the maximum allowed under Georgia state law. Name sometimes spelled 'Adrianna.' This is a fountain electrocution, not a dock/marina ESD incident.

Notes: Kyle Richard McGonigle, 36, of Vine Grove, Kentucky, was electrocuted on Monday night, May 20, 2013, at Rough River Lake near Peter Cave in Grayson County. McGonigle was swimming and fishing with three co-workers when he heard his friend's dog yelping in the water about five feet from a boat. He dove in to rescue the dog and both he and the dog drowned. Autopsy confirmed death from low-voltage electrocution. Two energized shore power cords had fallen into the water — one from a dock power pedestal, the second from a houseboat. Electrical inspection found multiple violations: spliced cords, non-functioning GFCI receptacles, shore power receptacles in poor condition, and broken covers. Alcohol was found at the scene.
Legal Outcome: Death cited as catalyst for Kentucky HB 107 (Samantha Chipley Act)
Electrical Source Detail: Two energized shore power cords fell into water; one from dock power pedestal, one from houseboat; coupling submerged
Fault Description: Two energized cords fell into water at coupling point; spliced cords, non-functioning GFCI receptacles, shore power receptacles in poor condition, broken covers
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as September 27, 2012; correct date is May 20, 2013 per obituary, news, and coroner; Date off by approximately 8 months and in the wrong year
Research Notes: ESDPA date of Sep 27, 2012 is wrong by 8 months and in wrong year. May 20, 2013 was a Monday, matching news reports of 'Monday night.' McGonigle was a Hardin County High School graduate and manager at Colton's Steakhouse in Radcliff. Two daughters: Alexus (Elizabethtown) and Kylee (Paducah). WAVE3 article says age 38 but all other sources say 36.

Notes: On Memorial Day (May 27, 2013), Gary Muter and his 6-year-old granddaughter Ava Muter were swimming approximately 10 feet from Evelyn Morrison's dock at Lake Lanier, Gainesville, GA. Gary felt a jolt in his elbow, recognized it as electric shock, grabbed Ava and got both out of the water. Morrison's electrician ruled out her dock as the source (it had GFCI protection). The actual source of the electrical current was never identified. A power outage occurred simultaneously on the street.
Electrical Source Detail: Gary Muter and 6-year-old granddaughter Ava were 10 feet from dock when Gary felt a jolt in his elbow; he grabbed Ava and both exited water safely
Fault Description: Source of electrical current never conclusively identified; dock owner's electrician ruled out Morrison's dock; dock had GFCI-like interrupter protection; simultaneous power outage on the street
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA describes victim as 'a man and his 6-year-old daughter' — actually grandfather and granddaughter (Gary and Ava Muter); 'Shocked three or more times' detail cannot be verified from independent source
Research Notes: ESDPA incorrectly states 'father and daughter' — independent source confirms grandfather and granddaughter. The 'three or more times' shock detail from ESDPA cannot be confirmed from the Gainesville Times article, which describes a single jolt. Lake Lanier has documented ESD incidents including the fatal July 2023 Thomas Milner electrocution.

Notes: On June 18, 2013, a woman in a flooded basement in Scott City, Missouri was shocked while unplugging cords. She rescued her cat and survived by floating on furniture. Non-marina context (flooded basement). No flooding event documented in Scott City for June 2013; date or location may be inaccurate.
Electrical Source Detail: Woman shocked while unplugging cords in flooded basement; survived by floating on furniture
Fault Description: Electrical cords submerged in floodwater in basement
ESDPA Data Issues: No documented flood event in Scott City on June 18, 2013 — date or location may be inaccurate; Similar incident (woman shocked in flooded basement, 2013) found in St. Joseph County (different state)
Research Notes: No independent sources found. No flood event documented for Scott City, MO on or around June 18, 2013. Major Missouri flooding occurred in August 2013, not June. A similar incident (woman shocked in flooded basement, 2013) occurred in St. Joseph County (different state). Possible date, location, or incident conflation. Scenario is technically plausible.

Notes: Kevin Curtis Short, 34, of Lily, KY, was electrocuted at a private dock on Lake Cumberland. A frayed extension cord energized the aluminum dock ladder. In his final moments, Short pushed his young son past the ladder to safety. He was a National Guard veteran and Walmart Distribution Center manager. His death was cited in the Kentucky legislature as motivation for House Bill 107 (Samantha Chipley Act).
Electrical Source Detail: Extension cord powering fish finder/light at dock; cord slipped behind aluminum ladder and frayed against sharp rock
Fault Description: Extension cord insulation frayed from rubbing against sharp rock; aluminum ladder became energized when victim's weight pushed it onto exposed conductor
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as May 20, 2013; correct date is June 30, 2013
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 41 days. Coroner Chuck Godbey ruled cause as low-voltage electrocution. LOA8 obituary mining research (March 2026) confirmed full name Kevin Short, 34, of Lily KY. National Guard veteran; Walmart distribution center manager. Multiple independent sources found: Lexington Herald-Leader, Sentinel-Echo, Firehouse.com, WMKY public radio, AuGres Maritime Museum. Short's death cited in Kentucky legislature for House Bill 107 (Samantha Chipley Act).

Notes: Man felt electric shock and paralysis while swimming off a docked boat at Lake Norman, NC in July 2013. He managed to move far enough away from the electrical source to regain control of his muscles. Classic ESD near-miss with paralysis followed by escape. Date is approximate (month only).
Electrical Source Detail: Man felt electric shock and paralysis while swimming off a docked boat; managed to move far enough away to regain muscle control
Fault Description: Unknown — classic ESD near-miss with paralysis followed by escape
Research Notes: No independent sources found. Lake Norman is a recognized ESD hazard location with numerous docks and marinas. Symptoms described (shock, paralysis, recovery after moving away) are classic ESD near-miss indicators. Approximate date suggests informal or secondhand reporting.

Notes: Daniel Petersen, 46, of Big Lake, MN, died July 7 after being electrocuted July 4, 2013, while attempting to charge his houseboat battery at Eagle Lake. He slipped while standing in 20 inches of water and immersed a plugged-in battery charger. His 49-year-old sister was also shocked attempting rescue but survived.
All Victims: Daniel Petersen (46, M); Unknown Petersen (49, F)
Electrical Source Detail: Plugged-in battery charger immersed in water when victim slipped
Fault Description: Victim was standing in 20 inches of water holding a plugged-in battery charger; slipped and immersed charger, causing severe electrical shock
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as June 30; correct date is July 4, 2013
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 4 days. Death date July 7, incident date July 4.

Notes: On July 11, 2013, eight college girls were swimming at the 35 mile marker on Lake of the Ozarks, MO. One girl's arm went numb. Others were shocked while exiting the water at a nearby dock. All survived without serious injury.
Electrical Source Detail: Eight college girls swimming at 35 mile marker; one girl's arm went numb; others shocked while exiting water at a nearby dock
Fault Description: Unknown — shock felt at dock exit point
Research Notes: No independent sources found despite extensive searching. The 35 mile marker is a real, high-density dock area on the main channel. Eight victims is a relatively large group, making the absence of any media coverage somewhat surprising. Lake of the Ozarks has documented fatal ESD incidents in 2012 and 2015.

Notes: Lauren Suzanne Cecil, 11, was electrocuted at Brookside Swim Club when a city-owned power line connector failed, causing the line to fall and energize the pool's metal ladder through saturated ground. Three girls were in the pool; two escaped via the concrete side, but Lauren grabbed the metal ladder. Not a classic dock/marina ESD — overhead power line failure affecting a pool.
Legal Outcome: Parents Lori and Keith Cecil filed suit against City of Lexington in Davidson County Superior Court.
Electrical Source Detail: Failed connector on power pole caused line to fall; current traveled through saturated ground to pool metal ladder
Fault Description: City-owned power line connector failed; downed line energized pool metal ladder via ground current through saturated soil
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 4, 2013; correct date is July 16, 2013
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 12 days. Victim was member of Brookside Swim Team and Girls on the Run.

Notes: Paige Scheel, 12, was electrocuted while filling an inflatable pool in her backyard in Syracuse, NY. She picked up an extension cord or electric pump plugged into an outlet and immediately began shaking, then fell face-first into the pool. A friend tried to pull her out but received a shock. She was pronounced dead at Upstate University Hospital.
Electrical Source Detail: Extension cord or electric pump plugged into outlet; contact while filling inflatable pool
Fault Description: Extension cord or electric pump contacted water; likely no GFCI protection on outdoor outlet
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 17; correct date is July 19, 2013
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 2 days. Parents: John Scheel and Bethany Davis Scheel. Recently graduated from Webster Elementary School.

Notes: Stephanie Diaz-Ruiz, 19, was babysitting when a severe storm knocked down power lines. She stepped outside to investigate a power outage and was electrocuted by current traveling through wet ground 25 feet from the downed line. First responders could not reach her until Georgia Power cut the power. Not a classic ESD case — downed power line on land, not dock/marina.
Electrical Source Detail: Storm-downed power line; current traveled 25 feet through wet ground/standing water
Fault Description: Downed power line after severe storm; current conducted through standing water/wet ground 25 feet to victim
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 19; correct date is July 24, 2013
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 5 days. Classified as overhead_line, not classic ESD.

Notes: Raul Hernandez Martinez, 27, died after rescuing his 11-year-old brother David Duran from the electrified pool at Hilton Houston Westchase on Labor Day weekend. Pool lights turned on around 7:40 PM, surging current through the water. Martinez fought through the current to push David to safety but was overcome. He was removed from life support September 6. Mother Maria Isabel Duran was knocked unconscious attempting rescue. Criminal charges filed against two electricians from Brown Electrical Inc.
All Victims: Raul Hernandez Martinez (27, M); David Duran (11, M); Maria Isabel Duran (F); Valeria Sanchez (F)
Legal Outcome: Two electricians (James Ray Pyle, Jason Joseph Gorczyka) charged with criminally negligent homicide. Settlement provided college funds and lifetime annuity for survivors.
Electrical Source Detail: Improper wiring to pool light by Brown Electrical Inc.; no GFCI protection; unpermitted work
Fault Description: Pool light improperly wired without permit; no GFCI protection; electrical code violations
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 25, 2013; correct date is August 31, 2013 (Labor Day weekend); Victim described as 'older man'; actually 27 years old
Research Notes: ESDPA date wrong by over a month. Victim was 27, not 'older man' as ESDPA described. LOA1-C news archive and LOA11 YouTube research confirmed extensive coverage by KHOU 11, KTRK/ABC13, and Click2Houston. Criminal charges against two Brown Electric electricians (Jason Gorczyca, James Pyle) for criminally negligent homicide. Civil settlement in April 2015 with Hilton Hotels and Brown Electric.

Notes: 7-year-old Calder Sloan was electrocuted in his family's pool at their North Miami home. He had celebrated his 7th birthday one week earlier. The pool light switch was not grounded and the grounding cable was loose. No permits had been pulled in the prior 12 months. A man performing CPR was also shocked when he put his hand in the pool. The Caleb & Calder's Awesome Foundation was established in his memory.
Legal Outcome: Wrongful death lawsuit filed; outcome not publicly available.
Electrical Source Detail: Pool light switch not properly grounded; grounding cable was loose/not installed
Fault Description: Pool light switch ungrounded; grounding cable not installed; no permits pulled or inspections done in prior 12 months
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as Sep 2013; correct date is April 13, 2014; ESDPA location listed as North Miami Beach; correct is North Miami
Research Notes: ESDPA date (Sep 2013) and location (North Miami Beach) are both wrong. Correct: April 13, 2014, North Miami FL. Mother: Carla Sloan; brother: Caleb.

Facility Name: Palms West Condominiums pool, 1950 W. 56th St.
Notes: On April 27, 2014, three children were shocked at the Palms West Condominiums pool in Hialeah, FL. Diego Cabrera (10), Daniella Gamez (6), and a 5-year-old girl were shocked by an improperly grounded pool pump. The 5-year-old touched the metal hand rail and was paralyzed by electricity. An adult man (believed to be her grandfather) attempted rescue and was also shocked but managed to pull her out. All three children were hospitalized at Miami Children's Hospital for four nights. Surveillance video captured the incident. Hialeah city inspectors blamed unpermitted and shoddy electrical work on pool equipment.
All Victims: Diego Cabrera (10, M); Daniella Gamez (6, F); Unknown (5, F); Unknown (M)
Electrical Source Detail: Improperly grounded pool pump; unpermitted and shoddy electrical work on pool equipment
Fault Description: Deficient wiring in pool pumps and lack of proper bonding and grounding in pool equipment; unpermitted electrical work
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA states 'two adults were shocked' — sources confirm at least one adult rescuer was shocked, but exact count of adults uncertain
Research Notes: Extensively covered by national and local media including ABC News, CBS Miami, Local10, and trade publications. Surveillance video of the incident was released. The exact number of adults shocked is unclear from sources — ESDPA claims two but sources confirm at least one rescuer.

Notes: CCTV footage captured children suffering electric shocks after touching a metal handrail in a Florida apartment swimming pool. One girl's body froze upon contact; her father pulled her free while getting shocked. A second girl remained paralyzed on the railing until her grandfather dragged her out and collapsed from the current. A 10-year-old boy was also shocked. All three children survived. Police blamed unconnected ground wires in the pool's pump house. The footage became one of the most-viewed ESD-related videos and is frequently used in awareness campaigns.
All Victims: Unknown (F); Unknown (F); Unknown (10, M); Unknown (M); Unknown (M)
Electrical Source Detail: Unconnected ground wires in pool pump house; energized metal handrail
Fault Description: Unconnected ground wires in pool pump house caused metal handrail in pool to become energized
Research Notes: Near-miss: all survived. Exact location within Florida not established. No victim names in any sources. Notable for dramatic CCTV footage widely used in ESD awareness campaigns.

Facility Name: Bullfrog Marina, Glen Canyon National Recreation Area
Notes: Alec McQueen, 22, of Salt Lake City, was electrocuted at Bullfrog Marina on Lake Powell. He jumped into the water from his boat's swim deck and was immediately overcome. A friend and two rescuers were also shocked. A boat in the adjacent slip had a 30-amp cord improperly inserted into a worn 50-amp outlet. McQueen's body was recovered two days later, 113 feet below the surface.
Legal Outcome: Family sued Aramark Sports and Entertainment Services (marina operator) and boat owner.
Electrical Source Detail: 30-amp power cord improperly inserted into worn 50-amp outlet in adjacent slip
Fault Description: Amperage mismatch — 30-amp cord inserted into worn 50-amp outlet, causing electricity to discharge into water
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as April 23; correct date is June 10, 2014
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by nearly 2 months. Marina operated by Aramark under NPS contract.

Notes: On July 4, 2014, several swimmers in a cove near the 7 Mile Marker at Lake of the Ozarks felt electric shock in the water. Turning off dock power didn't resolve the issue. The source was a live wire shorted to a ramp approximately 100 yards away, sending 120V AC into the water. The dock at the ramp had been disconnected years prior, but the electrical line was never properly disconnected from the main breaker. The breaker had been turned off and the wire wrapped around the ramp frame; someone later turned it back on, energizing the water for an estimated month before discovery. All swimmers recognized the danger and exited safely.
Electrical Source Detail: Live wire from improperly decommissioned dock ramp shorted to the ramp, sending 120V into water approximately 100 yards from where swimmers felt tingling
Fault Description: Dock had been disconnected years ago but electrical line was never disconnected from main breaker box; breaker was simply turned off and wire wrapped around ramp frame; someone later turned breaker back on, energizing ramp for estimated one month before discovery
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA describes source as 'abandoned boat ramp' — more precisely, it was an improperly decommissioned dock ramp with electrical service left connected
Research Notes: Well-documented by Lake Expo and EC&M. Nearly paralleled the fatal July 4, 2012 electrocution of Anderson children at the same lake. The one-month duration of energization before discovery highlights the hidden nature of ESD hazards.
All Sources Detail:
Lake Expo (news) https://www.lakeexpo.com/news/lake_news/swimmers-narrowly-escape-electrocution-in-lake-of-the-ozarks-cove/article_a2ed9992-085a-11e4-997b-0019bb2963f4.html [2014-07]
EC&M (Electrical Construction & Maintenance) (news) https://www.ecmweb.com/safety/shock-electrocution/article/20899146/swimmers-report-shock-in-lake-of-the-ozarks-cove [2014-07]
Lake Expo (news) https://www.lakeexpo.com/boating/the_lake/stop-dock-shock-new-coalition-at-the-lake-raises-the-bar-for-electricians/article_3760b108-914b-11e9-9c42-3710bc1712cb.html [2019-06]

Notes: Andrew Orvis, 8, was electrocuted at Piney Shores Resort on Lake Conroe. He sat on the dock and dangled his feet in the water, then fell in with unnatural body motion. In the water approximately 15 seconds before his father pulled him out and performed CPR. He was airlifted to Memorial Hermann Hospital where he died. Harris County ME confirmed electrocution Sept 17, 2014; drowning was NOT a contributing cause.
Legal Outcome: Lawsuit filed against Silverleaf Resorts seeking $1M+. Silverleaf blamed electrical installer.
Electrical Source Detail: Broken conduit with bare electrified wires on dock
Fault Description: Dock conduit broken in places with exposed energized wires; dock lighting installation was defective
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as June 10; correct date is July 20, 2014
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 40 days. Electrocution confirmed as sole cause — drowning explicitly ruled out.

Notes: Madeline 'Maddy' Roskie, 18, died while inner-tubing on the Puyallup River. She complained of leg numbness then lost consciousness. Her boyfriend also felt tingling. Death certificate listed cause as 'undetermined,' though a PSE technician later found 280V from an ungrounded irrigation pump on adjacent property. Father Earl Roskie filed lawsuit against Puget Sound Energy.
Electrical Source Detail: Ungrounded irrigation pump extending into river on adjacent property; 280V to ground measured
Fault Description: Irrigation pump on adjacent property was ungrounded; PSE technician found 280V to ground 20 days after incident
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 20; correct date is August 2, 2014
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 13 days. Official cause of death listed as undetermined despite strong circumstantial evidence of electrocution.
All Sources Detail:
The Seattle Times (news) https://www.seattletimes.com/seattle-news/family-sues-pse-saying-daughter-18-was-electrocuted-while-tubing-the-puyallup-river/ [2016-03-15]
Legacy.com / The News Tribune (obituary) https://www.legacy.com/obituaries/tribnet/obituary.aspx?pid=172054289 [2014-08-05]

Notes: Sarah Grace Tellifero, 13, daughter of Pastor Jon Tellifero, was electrocuted at Lake Bruin when a boat lift malfunction energized a pontoon boat's ladder. A 17-year-old was also shocked but survived. Other swimmers had previously reported tingling in the water — an unrecognized warning sign.
All Victims: Sarah Grace Tellifero (13, F); Unknown (17, F)
Electrical Source Detail: Boat lift motor malfunction caused pontoon boat to act as conductor
Fault Description: Malfunctioning boat lift motor energized pontoon boat's metal ladder; swimmers had previously reported tingling
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as Aug 2; correct date is Aug 24, 2014
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 22 days. LOA8 obituary mining research (March 2026) confirmed full details: Sarah Grace Tellifero, daughter of Rev. Jon Tellifero and Beverly Tellifero (First United Methodist Church West Monroe). Incident occurred during church youth retreat at private home on Lake Bruin. Multiple independent sources: Shreveport Times, Ouachita Citizen, Mulhearn Funeral Home obituary, Scuttlebutt Sailing News, Waterhawk Technologies. Tensas Parish Sheriff Rickey Jones investigated.

Notes: Dianne Guyton was swimming in Smith Lake, Alabama, approximately 2015, when she reached up to the dock to get her phone and was electrocuted by electricity in the water. Her neighbors brought her to shore. She survived but nearly drowned. As of June 2024, she was publicly sharing her story to warn others about ESD risks. Smith Lake is the same lake where Carmen Johnson died in April 2016 (ESD-2016-04-16-1) — Guyton's incident may have preceded Johnson's death by approximately one year.
Electrical Source Detail: Dock electrical fault — electricity in water near dock
Fault Description: Electricity in water near dock electrocuted swimmer reaching up to dock
Research Notes: Date approximate ('nearly nine years ago' from June 2024 report = c.2015). Near-miss survival case. Single source (WBRC TV interview). Same lake as fatal Carmen Johnson incident one year later. Further research needed to establish exact date and whether ESD was officially documented.

Facility Name: Rental property dock, Woods Hollow Cove, 22.2 Mile Marker
Notes: Marcus Colburn, 21, of East Moline, IL, died on Father's Day while swimming at a rental property dock on Lake of the Ozarks. Both Colburn and Taylor Curley felt electricity in the water. When Colburn grabbed the dock ladder, he was shocked and fell back in face-down. Someone shut off power, saving Curley. Circuit breaker had been tripping repeatedly — a warning sign that went unaddressed.
Electrical Source Detail: Faulty junction box between dock and residence at rental property
Fault Description: Faulty junction box; circuit breaker tripping repeatedly every 10-15 seconds (warning sign ignored)
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA ESD section date is Aug 24, 2014; correct date is June 21, 2015; Near Miss section had correct date of June 21, 2015
Research Notes: ESDPA ESD section date wrong by 10 months. Incident at rental property highlights lack of electrical maintenance at vacation rentals.

Notes: Dr. Eric Jonathan Hughes, 37, a Tuscaloosa orthodontist, was found in approximately 4 feet of water near his pontoon boat in Lake Tuscaloosa after going swimming to cool off from cutting grass. Investigators determined electric shock contributed to drowning. Lake Tuscaloosa had multiple ESD incidents, including two more deaths in 2017.
Electrical Source Detail: Specific source not publicly determined; boat, dock, or other equipment suspected
Fault Description: Investigators determined electric shock contributed to drowning; exact source not publicly announced
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as June 21, 2015; correct date is August 8, 2015
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by nearly 2 months. Lake Tuscaloosa is a known ESD hotspot.

Notes: On August 25, 2015, Joel St. Jean, a commercial diver in his mid-20s from Portsmouth, NH, was shocked while cleaning the bottom of a 36-foot Catalina sailboat at Portsmouth Naval Shipyard marina (physically located in Kittery, Maine). The sailboat was connected to shore power. St. Jean was found floating unconscious with his scuba regulator still in his mouth. He suffered prolonged headaches, memory loss, and fatigue. He filed a $500,000 lawsuit against the U.S. government alleging negligent maintenance of the marina's electrical supply.
Legal Outcome: Joel St. Jean filed $500,000 lawsuit against U.S. government under admiralty law; Justice Department sought dismissal; case scheduled for mediation in 2016; outcome unknown
Electrical Source Detail: Diver was cleaning bottom of a 36-foot Catalina sailboat connected to shore electrical power at the marina; found unconscious floating with scuba regulator still in mouth
Fault Description: Alleged faulty wiring and negligent maintenance of marina electrical supply; government disputed the claim
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA lists location as 'Portsmouth NH' — shipyard is physically in Kittery, Maine (though named Portsmouth Naval Shipyard)
Research Notes: Shipyard is physically in Kittery, ME despite the name. The government disputed the faulty wiring allegation. Lawsuit outcome not publicly available — may require PACER search. This was a workplace incident (commercial diving) unlike most ESD near-misses.

Facility Name: Private residence, 2300 block of Yosemite Drive
Notes: Jim Tramel, 43, VP of Sales at RevJet from Burlingame CA, died on Easter Sunday after jumping into an electrified pool in Palm Springs to save his daughter. Seven people total were shocked at the family gathering. The home was built in 1963 with outdated pool electrical systems. His daughter survived but was in critical condition.
All Victims: Jim Tramel (43, M); Unknown Tramel (9, F)
Electrical Source Detail: Faulty pool wiring in 1963-era home
Fault Description: Faulty wiring in pool system of home built in 1963; no functioning GFCI protection
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date wrong: Aug 8, 2015; correct is March 27, 2016; ESDPA state wrong: listed as Florida; correct is California; Daughter survived (critical condition), not confirmed dead as ESDPA suggested
Research Notes: ESDPA had wrong date (off by ~7.5 months), wrong state (FL vs CA), and wrong outcome for daughter.

Notes: Carmen Elizabeth Johnson, 15, a Priceville High School cheerleader, was electrocuted at her family's lakehouse dock on Smith Lake. She and friend Reagan Gargis jumped off the top deck into electrically charged water. Her father Jimmy and brother Zach also jumped in and felt 'piercing electric shocks.' Jimmy yelled to his wife to cut power. Carmen died April 17. The Johnson family became ESD awareness advocates.
All Victims: Carmen Johnson (15, F); Reagan Gargis (F); Jimmy Johnson (M); Zach Johnson (M)
Electrical Source Detail: Water filled an electrical box on the boat dock; GFCI not functioning
Fault Description: Water-filled electrical box on dock sent current through metal dock components; GFCI breaker was not working

Research Notes: ESDPA ESD date off by 20 days. Incident April 16; death April 17.

Facility Name: Aztec Motel, 400 block of East Lavender Road
Notes: Greg Subiszak, 34, of Fair Lawn NJ, was found unconscious at the bottom of the Aztec Motel pool on Memorial Day weekend. Teenager Ivan Martinez jumped in to save him but went into convulsions from electrical shock. A maintenance worker eventually cut the electricity. Subiszak later died from his injuries. Police determined no criminal charges were warranted.
All Victims: Greg Subiszak (34, M); Ivan Christopher Martinez (17, M)
Electrical Source Detail: Electrical short in lamp post/light fixture near the pool
Fault Description: Electrical short in a light fixture near the pool energized the water; pool had been inspected 3 weeks prior
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as April 16; correct date is May 28, 2016; ESDPA said 'pool drain suspected' — incorrect; cause was electrical short in light fixture; ESDPA said 'last known critical' — victim actually died

Notes: An 8-year-old girl was among 8 children swimming when a child flipped the pool light switch, sending current into the water. She was the only one unable to get out. Family began CPR; police used AED; she was airlifted to Penn State Hershey Medical Center in critical condition. Seven other children were treated on scene. Final outcome not confirmed in available sources — classified as fatal per ESDPA listing but may have survived.
Electrical Source Detail: Faulty pool light circuit
Fault Description: Pool light circuit fault; when light switch was turned on by a child, electrical current entered the pool
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA ESD section date listed as June 27; correct date is May 29, 2016 (Memorial Day weekend); Final outcome unknown — last reported in critical condition
Research Notes: ESDPA ESD date off by about 1 month. Victim name not released (minor). Outcome uncertain — listed as fatal but last known status was critical condition.

Notes: Two adults drowned at a private dock on Chickamauga Lake, Sale Creek, Hamilton County TN. Randy Freeney (62) entered the water and was incapacitated; Donna Berger (34), his neighbor, jumped in to rescue him and also drowned. Freeney's wife also attempted to help but was pulled back. Son made 911 call. Classic ESD pattern: two victims simultaneously incapacitated in same water near powered dock, with skilled swimmer (Berger) unable to function after entering water. No electrical investigation was documented in any public record. WSOC-TV Charlotte used this incident in a 9 Investigates ESD documentary segment.
All Victims: Donna Berger (34, F); Randy Freeney (62, M)
Fault Description: No electrical investigation documented in public records
ESDPA Data Issues: Listed as near-miss (NM-2016-001) but was actually a double fatality; ESDPA date listed as May 29 or July 3, 2016; actual date was June 17, 2016; ESDPA stated 'All survived' but both adults died; Location listed as 'a Tennessee Lake (exact location unknown)' — actually Chickamauga Lake, Sale Creek
Research Notes: Not in ESDPA list. This is a classic ESD rescue pattern — primary victim incapacitated near dock, rescuer enters same water and is also incapacitated. The absence of any electrical investigation is itself notable. LOA8 found multiple independent obituary sources. Remains SUSPECTED because no electrical fault was ever confirmed or denied — the standard 'no foul play' determination was made without electrical testing.
