Facility Name: Private residence, near 43rd Avenue and Thomas Road
Notes: A 27-year-old father was electrocuted while attempting to change a pool light. His wife and three children witnessed the incident and were also shocked when trying to rescue him. His 8-year-old daughter reported he sank in the deep end. Official cause classified as drowning with electrocution as contributing factor. Victim name not released.
Electrical Source Detail: Pool light fixture — victim was attempting to change/replace it while in the pool
Fault Description: Pool light electrified water while victim was changing it; wife and children also shocked during rescue attempt
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as May 28; correct date is June 27, 2016
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 1 month. Phoenix Fire recommended leaving pool electrical work to licensed professionals.

Notes: A 24-year-old construction worker employed by LHC Construction Management, LLC, was electrocuted at approximately 4:00 PM while installing tongue-and-groove wood trim on the ceiling of a new dock area under construction. He came into contact with an energized boat lift, received an electric shock, fell into the water, and drowned. This is mechanistically identical to ESD: electrical current from dock infrastructure incapacitates a person who then drowns.
Electrical Source Detail: Energized boat lift at new dock construction site
Fault Description: Worker installing trim on new dock ceiling contacted energized boat lift; received shock and fell into water; drowned
Research Notes: Occupational fatality — ESD mechanism but worker context. OSHA Accident Summary Nr 87086.015. Location not specified in OSHA summary beyond 'dock area.' NAICS 236118 (New Single-Family Housing Construction). Boat lift as energized source is a documented common ESD hazard.

Facility Name: Heritage Point community pool, Valley Forge Road
Notes: Rachel Rosoff, 17, an Enloe High School senior working as a lifeguard through Aquatic Management Group, was electrocuted at Heritage Point community pool on Labor Day weekend. She dipped her hand in the water to check chemicals. The grounding wire hadn't been inspected in 37 years. The Rosoff family became advocates for mandatory pool electrical inspections.
Legal Outcome: Case settled for $6 million. Pool maintenance company had warned about dangerous pump before the incident.
Electrical Source Detail: Pool pump motor failed; severed underground grounding conductor (damaged by corrosion over 37 years)
Fault Description: Pool pump motor failure combined with severed underground grounding conductor corroded over 37 years; wrong size capacitor installed by repair company
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 3; correct date is September 3, 2016 (Labor Day weekend)
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 2 months. Pool built to 1978 NEC; some rewiring in 2011 should have triggered inspection. ESDPA footnote explicitly excludes this from their marina list but acknowledges it. LOA11 YouTube research found 4 videos (2016-2024) including TMJ4/WRAL, CBS 17, WCNC, and WRAL follow-up. LOA3 legal records confirm $6M settlement.

Facility Name: Private residence, 17200 block of Madison Road
Notes: Sandra Sharp, 54, was found unconscious in her flooded basement at 4:28 AM on Sept 10. CPR restored a pulse; she was hospitalized but taken off life support Sept 12. A box fan was found nearby but police called this 'speculative.' Not a dock/marina ESD — indoor flooded basement.
Electrical Source Detail: Box fan in 6 inches of basement flood water (speculative per police)
Fault Description: Short circuit in flooded basement; box fan found near victim but causation speculative per police
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as Sep 3; correct incident date is Sep 10, 2016 (death Sep 12); ESDPA state listed as Iowa (IA); correct is Indiana (IN)
Research Notes: ESDPA had wrong date (off 7 days) and wrong state (Iowa vs Indiana). Death not immediate — took 2 days.

Notes: On October 7, 2016, a construction worker operating an industrial concrete saw contacted an energized electrical line in Shiloh, Illinois. He was rendered unconscious in approximately 6 inches of standing water. A rescuer was also shocked attempting to move the victim. Two Shiloh IL police officers used a canvas tie-down strap to extract the victim from the electrified water and performed CPR until paramedics arrived. The victim survived. The officers received a Life Saving Award from the Village Board of Trustees. Police Chief Jim Stover stated the victim would not have survived without the officers' training.
Electrical Source Detail: Industrial concrete saw contacted an energized electrical line; worker rendered unconscious in approximately 6 inches of standing water
Fault Description: Concrete saw contacted energized electrical line; no GFCI protection (regulatory violation for construction site)
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA lists location as 'Shiloh MO' — correct location is Shiloh, Illinois (St. Clair County); ESDPA states 'second worker was shocked' — actually a rescuer attempting to move the victim
Research Notes: ESDPA incorrectly lists location as Shiloh, MO — confirmed as Shiloh, IL by reference to Village Board of Trustees, police department, and Chief Jim Stover. Non-marina incident (construction site). Concrete saw was likely not GFCI-protected, a regulatory violation.

Body of Water: River (specific river withheld in CDC report)
Facility Name: Marina on a river (name withheld by MIFACE to protect privacy)
Notes: In summer 2017, a male marina owner in his 80s drowned after falling into six-foot-deep water at a boat slip in his own marina on a river in Michigan. His spouse described him as a 'good swimmer' who had fallen in at least twice before without incident (when others were present to assist). On the day of his death he was working alone. He was found floating upright with the crown of his head visible above the water. Tools and keys were in the ignition of a nearby boat. The critical finding from the CDC FACE report (17MI208, published March 9, 2020) is that neither the sheriff nor the medical examiner investigator checked the water for stray electrical current. The combination of good swimmer, familiar marina environment, active shore power infrastructure, and absence of any ESD investigation makes this a textbook case of potential ESD misclassification.
Electrical Source Detail: Marina with active shore power infrastructure; no electrical investigation conducted per CDC FACE report
Fault Description: No electrical investigation was conducted — neither the responding sheriff nor the medical examiner investigator checked the water for stray electrical current
Research Notes: Found via LOA2 (SUSP-2017-020). Specific location withheld by MIFACE to protect privacy. Exact date withheld (only 'Summer 2017'). Victim not named. The CDC FACE report focused on personal flotation device recommendations and working-alone protocols — not electrical safety. This case was found through government occupational fatality records, not through press coverage, meaning it likely never received public scrutiny as a potential ESD incident.

Notes: On Friday, March 24, 2017, a 59-year-old male fell into the water at a marina dock in Monmouth Beach, New Jersey, while attempting to board or disembark from a boat. A 51-year-old female friend jumped in to rescue him. Both drowned. The rescuer-drowning pattern is a classic ESD indicator. However, a critical limiting factor is water salinity: Monmouth Beach borders the Shrewsbury River, a tidal inlet with brackish to saltwater composition, particularly in March. ESD is not possible in saltwater because saltwater conducts electricity around (not through) the body. The March date also raises hypothermia and cold shock as alternative explanations for rapid incapacitation.
Electrical Source Detail: Marina dock with likely shore power infrastructure; no electrical investigation documented
Fault Description: No electrical investigation documented; water salinity at location (Shrewsbury River tidal inlet) may preclude traditional ESD mechanism
Research Notes: Found via LOA2 (SUSP-2017-021). Source is the Robson Forensic Expert Witness appendix on marina drownings — a litigation-support document. No independent news coverage located for this incident. Verification would require Monmouth County ME records and determination of water salinity at the specific dock location. The SUSP file identified the victims as John LoBello and Deborah Hollen-Lappin, but those names could not be independently verified in open sources.

Notes: Brothers Alex Lopez (12) and Isaiah Lopez (11) were electrocuted at Oakland Lake Park in Fort Worth. Alex stepped on a downed power line hidden in overgrown brush over 12 hours after morning storms. A friend told Isaiah, who went to check on his brother and was electrocuted when he touched Alex. Not a classic dock/marina ESD — downed power line incident.
All Victims: Alex Lopez (12, M); Isaiah Lopez (11, M)
Legal Outcome: Family sued Oncor Electric Delivery seeking up to $40M in damages.
Electrical Source Detail: Downed power line hidden in overgrown brush; lines were still energized 12+ hours after morning storms
Fault Description: Storm-downed power lines obscured by vegetation; Oncor did not respond for 12+ hours; lines still energized and arcing when firefighters arrived
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date wrong by ~18 months: listed as Sep 10, 2016; correct is March 29, 2017
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by ~18 months. Full names: Jose Alexandro Luis Lopez and Isaiah Alexander Luis Lopez. Both attended International Leadership of Texas school.

Notes: Two University of Alabama School of Law attorneys, Shelly Darling (34, Elder Law Clinic) and Elizabeth Whipple (41, Domestic Violence Clinic), died after going to sunbathe at a lakehouse dock on Lake Tuscaloosa. They went missing Friday evening; bodies recovered Saturday ~4:30 AM. A deputy was jolted by electricity on the dock during recovery. Autopsies confirmed electrocution. Lake Tuscaloosa had multiple ESD incidents (see also ESD-2015-08-08-1).
All Victims: Shelly Darling (34, F); Elizabeth Whipple (41, F)
Electrical Source Detail: Dock/pier electrified via boat lift power supply — specific fault not publicly determined
Fault Description: Dock found to be electrified; police officer shocked at dock; divers observed arcing between police boat and dock; likely boat lift power supply
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as March 29; correct is April 14, 2017
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 16 days. Second ESD-related death(s) at Lake Tuscaloosa after Dr. Hughes in 2015. LOA11 YouTube research found video of Capt. Kip Hart press conference confirming electrocution ruling and arcing between police boat and dock. LOA8 obituary mining confirmed victim identities.

Notes: 23-year-old man reportedly electrocuted when he extended an aluminum pole into the water around his sailboat at a private dock in Mandeville, LA. No independent sources could be located to verify this incident.
Electrical Source Detail: Unknown; aluminum pole extended into water around sailboat at private dock
Fault Description: Cause unknown; water may have been electrified or victim may have made direct contact with electrified object
ESDPA Data Issues: Date uncertain (May 22 vs May 24, 2017); No independent sources found to verify any details
Research Notes: UNVERIFIED — no independent news, obituary, or official sources found despite extensive searching. Only source is ESDPA list itself.

Facility Name: Private residence — basement with sump pump
Notes: On May 1, 2017, a homeowner in Rochester, New York rigged a sump pump and received a significant shock in his legs. A motor fault was the cause. No GFCI protection was in place. Non-marina context (residential basement).
Electrical Source Detail: Homeowner rigged a sump pump and received significant shock in legs; motor fault was the cause
Fault Description: Motor fault in DIY-rigged sump pump; no GFCI protection
Research Notes: No independent sources found. Non-fatal residential incident with no hospitalization unlikely to generate media coverage. Date may be approximate.

Facility Name: De la Gappe Boulevard, Touraines neighbourhood — flooded street
Notes: On May 6, 2017, Richard Lafontaine was hospitalized in critical condition after suffering electric shock while building a sandbag barrier to protect homes on De la Gappe Boulevard in Gatineau, Quebec, during the catastrophic 2017 Ottawa River flooding. He fell into electrified floodwater on Saturday afternoon. Bystanders administered CPR and he was revived at the scene. As of May 12, he remained in hospital in serious condition. The water was electrified by electrical equipment in the flooded area. No GFCI protection was in place. His neighbor Diane Parisien, whom he was helping, participated in the rescue.
Electrical Source Detail: Fell into electrified floodwater while building sandbag barrier during 2017 Ottawa River flooding; electrical equipment (likely pumps) in floodwater was the source
Fault Description: No GFCI protection on electrical equipment operating in floodwater; specific source not identified but pumps in use during flood response
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date is May 8, 2017 — correct date is May 6, 2017; ESDPA location is 'Ottawa, Canada' — correct location is Gatineau, Quebec (a separate city in a different province); ESDPA describes cause as 'contacted a cord powering a submersible pump' — CBC reports describe him falling into electrified floodwater while building sandbag barrier
Research Notes: ESDPA has multiple errors: wrong date (May 8 vs May 6), wrong city (Ottawa vs Gatineau, QC), and imprecise cause description. Canadian incident. Life-threatening severity — required CPR at scene, critical condition on admission. Context of major Ottawa River flooding event. Non-marina context (flooded residential street).

Notes: Carl McGrady, 65, was electrocuted entering his pool to clean it on May 19. His son Lucas, 35, jumped in to rescue him and was also electrocuted. Pam McGrady was shocked helping pull Carl out but survived. Carl died that evening; Lucas died June 2 after 14 days on life support at UAB Medical Center. Lucas was a father of three, engaged, and owned M&M Lawncare.
All Victims: Carl McGrady (65, M); Lucas McGrady (35, M); Pamela McGrady (F)
Electrical Source Detail: Electrical short in pool; specific source not publicly determined
Fault Description: Pool electrified by electrical short; specific fault not publicly announced
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA ESD section date April 14; correct is May 19, 2017

Notes: Aldo Jordani Rojas, 14, was electrocuted after stepping into a storm-flooded puddle containing a downed power line in Laredo, TX. ESDPA incorrectly states two brothers were killed — only one victim died. The 'two brothers' detail likely comes from the separate Fort Worth/Lopez brothers incident (FI-2016-001/ESD-2017-03-29-1). Not a dock/marina ESD — downed power line incident.
Electrical Source Detail: Storm-downed power line in flooded field near victim's home
Fault Description: Severe storm downed power lines; live wire electrified puddle in field where victim frequently played
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as May 19; correct is May 21, 2017; ESDPA says two brothers killed; only ONE victim in Laredo (possibly conflated with Fort Worth Lopez brothers)
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 2 days. CRITICAL: ESDPA conflated this with the Lopez brothers incident. Only ONE victim in Laredo.

Notes: Evan Currie, 19, a Xavier University sophomore from Dublin OH, was Ohio's first reported electric shock drowning victim. After docking at Miller Marina and plugging in shore power, the family dog fell in the water. Father Jeffrey entered and struggled; both sons jumped in. Evan's mother unplugged shore power, stopping the current. Evan was unresponsive, convulsing, and shouted 'electricity' between convulsions. Both boat and marina power were inspected and cleared.
Electrical Source Detail: Shore power connection; both boat and shore power checked out and were properly grounded — source undetermined
Fault Description: Electrical fault source undetermined after investigation; boat and shore power both passed inspection
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA ESD section date May 22, 2017; NM section June 17; correct is June 16, 2017
Research Notes: ESDPA ESD date off by 25 days. First ESD death ever reported in Ohio per ODNR.

Notes: Kayla Matos, 11, from Newark NJ, was visiting friends in Toms River when she was electrocuted in a lagoon. She and two friends were using an inflatable raft near a dormant boat lift with corroded electrical equipment. When the raft contacted the lift rail, Kayla touched the energized metal. All three girls were wearing life jackets. Adults were present and began CPR. She died at Community Medical Center. She was a 5th grader at Ann Street School and a Girl Scout.
Electrical Source Detail: Dormant boat lift with corroded electrical junction box; energized when raft contacted rail
Fault Description: Boat lift unused for years had corroded electrical junction box; energized metal rail
Research Notes: ESDPA date correct (June 17, 2017). One of the few correctly dated entries. LOA11 YouTube research found CBS New York and ABC News video coverage confirming victim name and mechanism. LOA1-E news archive research independently confirmed.

Facility Name: Crab Corner, Menemsha Beach / transient dock
Notes: On June 21, 2017, several West Tisbury third graders on a school beach outing felt a 'charge' while swimming or wading at Crab Corner, Menemsha Beach, Chilmark, Martha's Vineyard, MA. Children experienced a 'small shock feeling' but were all fine. Harbormaster Dennis Jason confirmed the tingle when he reached his hand in the water. Power was cut at an electrical box on the bulkhead above the dock. The Town of Chilmark closed the swimming area. Follow-up investigation identified faulty grounding work from a contractor 5-8 years prior on the transient dock as the suspected source. Repairs were scheduled for July 12, 2017. The problem was described as 'recurrent.'
Electrical Source Detail: Faulty grounding work on transient dock performed by contractor 5-8 years prior; recurrent 'tickle' of marine electricity
Fault Description: Grounding work executed by contractor 5-8 years ago (circa 2009-2012) on the transient dock; recurrent electrical leakage into water
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date is June 27, 2017 (the article publication date); actual incident date was June 21, 2017; ESDPA states cause was unknown — follow-up reporting identified suspected cause as faulty grounding on transient dock
Research Notes: ESDPA date of June 27 was the article publication date, not the incident date. Cause was later identified as faulty grounding work, not 'unknown' as ESDPA stated. Saltwater context. The recurrent nature of the problem suggests ongoing electrical leakage that had occurred before.
All Sources Detail:
Martha's Vineyard Times (news) https://www.mvtimes.com/2017/06/27/menemsha-beach-closed-precaution-tickle/ [2017-06-27]
Martha's Vineyard Times (news) https://www.mvtimes.com/2017/07/10/potential-fix-pending-voltage-leaching-dock-menemsha/ [2017-07-10]

Notes: Five people died at a water park in Akyazi, Turkey: three children (12, 15, 17) were caught in electrical current in the pool; the 58-year-old park manager and his 30-year-old son jumped in to save them and were also killed. Two others were injured touching energized metal railings. The park lacked a residual current breaker. Included in ESDPA list because 'this could have happened in the USA.'
All Victims: Unknown (12); Unknown (15); Unknown (17); Unknown (58, M); Unknown (30, M)
Electrical Source Detail: Pool lacked residual current breaker (RCD/GFCI equivalent)
Fault Description: Pool lacked residual current breaker; metal railings near pool also energized
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as June 18; most sources indicate June 23, 2017; ESDPA said 1 injured; actually 2 injured (from touching metal railings)
Research Notes: International incident. ESDPA date off by 5 days. Injury count was 2, not 1.

Notes: On July 3, 2017, a man's son grabbed the crossmember of a boat lift while under a boat at Lake Keowee, South Carolina. He was shocked but recovered. The boat lift was not properly grounded.
Electrical Source Detail: Boy grabbed crossmember of a boat lift while under a boat and was shocked; boat lift was not properly grounded
Fault Description: Improperly grounded boat lift
Research Notes: No independent sources found. Non-fatal outcome with quick recovery reduces likelihood of news coverage. Private property incident involving a minor may not have been reported. Lake Keowee spans Oconee and Pickens counties.

Notes: On July 7, 2017, two kayakers felt an electrical tingle at Canyon Lake, California. Authorities were investigating. The cause was unknown at the time of reporting.
Electrical Source Detail: Two kayakers felt electrical tingle in the water; cause was under investigation
Fault Description: Unknown — authorities were investigating at time of report
ESDPA Data Issues: Possible location ambiguity — could be Canyon Lake, California or Canyon Lake, Texas
Research Notes: No independent sources found. Canyon Lake, CA is a private, gated community. Non-serious outcome (tingle with no injury) reduces news coverage likelihood. Private community may handle incidents internally. Location could possibly be Canyon Lake, TX instead of CA.

Notes: Madison Coe, 14, from Lubbock TX, was electrocuted while taking a bath at her father's home in Lovington, NM. She was using her phone (Samsung S6 Edge) plugged into a charger cord connected to a frayed extension cord in a non-GFCI outlet. She tried to keep connections dry but was unaware of the cord's frayed section. CPSC was involved in the investigation. Family released her final text/photo to raise awareness.
Electrical Source Detail: Phone charger plugged into frayed extension cord plugged into non-GFCI, non-grounded outlet
Fault Description: Samsung S6 Edge phone charger cord → extension cord (with significant fraying) → non-GFCI, non-grounded bathroom outlet; victim contacted frayed area while in bathtub
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as June 23; correct date is July 9, 2017
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 16 days. Not classic ESD — bathtub electrocution from faulty extension cord.

Notes: On July 23, 2017, Cameron Brooks (28) of Spallumcheen, BC attempted to rescue his two dogs from an electrified creek on his property. The dogs died immediately upon entering the water. Brooks waded in up to his thighs before the current overwhelmed him; he fell backwards and pulled himself out. He suffered leg burns and was airlifted to Kelowna General Hospital. Fire Chief Dave Cummings stated he would likely have died if he had gone deeper. The source was a faulty submersible irrigation pump. Wife April and two young children (Aemon, 2; Armoury, 6 months) were also at the property.
Electrical Source Detail: Faulty submersible irrigation pump energized creek on private property; two dogs died immediately upon entering water
Fault Description: Faulty wiring in submersible irrigation pump sent electrical current through creek
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date is July 24, 2017 — correct date is July 23, 2017; ESDPA location is 'Kelowna, BC' — Kelowna was where victim was airlifted for treatment; incident occurred in Spallumcheen; ESDPA says 'pond pump' — correct source was an irrigation pump
Research Notes: ESDPA location of 'Kelowna' is where victim was airlifted, not where incident occurred (Spallumcheen). Date off by one day. Pump was irrigation, not pond pump. Canadian incident. Community fundraiser organized for the family. Non-marina context.
All Sources Detail:
CBC News (news) https://www.cbc.ca/news/canada/british-columbia/b-c-man-jumps-into-creek-to-save-dogs-suffers-electric-shock-1.4221434 [2017-07-26]
Castanet.net (Vernon) (news) https://www.castanet.net/news/Vernon/202575/Shocking-tale-of-survival [2017-07-27]
Castanet.net (Vernon) (news) https://www.castanet.net/news/Vernon/202695/Fundraiser-for-family [2017-07-31]
Vernon Morning Star (news) https://www.vernonmorningstar.com/news/family-struggles-after-electrocution/ [2017-07-30]

Notes: Three Boy Scouts from Troop 620, Hallsville TX, were killed during a sailing lesson on Lake O' The Pines when their Hobie Cat catamaran's 30-foot mast struck a 7,200-volt overhead transmission line operated by Upshur Rural Electric Cooperative. The catamaran caught fire. Will Brannon (18) and Heath Faucheaux (16) died at the scene. Thomas Larry (11) was airlifted to Shreveport hospital and died August 7. All three were members of the Hallsville High School band. NESC requires 37.5 ft minimum clearance for lakes over 2,000 acres suitable for sailing; Lake O' The Pines is 18,680 acres. A nearly identical incident killed attorney G. Brockett Irwin at the same lake in December 1982.
All Victims: Will Brannon (18, M); Heath Faucheaux (16, M); Thomas Larry (11, M)
Electrical Source Detail: Hobie Cat catamaran 30-foot mast struck 7,200-volt overhead transmission line operated by Upshur Rural Electric Cooperative
Fault Description: Overhead transmission power line crossing lake at insufficient clearance per NESC Standard 232-1 (required 37.5 ft minimum for lake over 2,000 acres; Lake O' The Pines is 18,680 acres)
Research Notes: ESD-ADJACENT: overhead power line strike, not dock/marina ESD. Not in ESDPA standard list. Texas Parks & Wildlife investigation records reportedly kept secret (FOIFT). The 1982 precursor incident establishes the utility knew of the hazard 35 years earlier. U.S. Army Corps of Engineers had jurisdiction over the lake.
All Sources Detail:

Notes: Cole R. Grad, 15, a Chiawana High School student, was electrocuted while swimming at a private irrigation pump station on the Columbia River. He contacted an energized pipe connected to a faulty pump system. Two firefighters were also shocked during rescue. Coroner ruled death accidental from low voltage electrocution.
Electrical Source Detail: Faulty wiring at pump house; cable went to ground energizing motor case
Fault Description: Cable at pump station went to ground, energizing outside motor case; breaker couldn't trip due to wiring fault
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 12; correct date is August 10, 2017
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by ~30 days. ESDPA spelled city as 'Pascoe'; correct is 'Pasco'.

Facility Name: Greens Bayou near Interstate 10, east Houston
Notes: During Hurricane Harvey, volunteer rescuers and two Daily Mail journalists (7 total) were on a rescue boat that was pushed into downed power lines in Greens Bayou. Two confirmed dead (Yahir Vizueth, 25; Jorge Perez, 31), two missing/presumed dead (Benjamin Vizueth, 31; Gustavo Rodriguez-Hernandez, 40). Three survivors clung to trees for 18 hours. The group had successfully rescued 7 people before the tragedy.
All Victims: Yahir Vizueth (25, M); Jorge Perez (31, M); Jose Vizueth (30, M)
Electrical Source Detail: Low-hanging downed power lines in floodwater; boat was pushed into lines by strong current
Fault Description: Hurricane Harvey floodwaters; boat caught in strong current and pushed into downed power lines
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA said 5 men; actually 7 (5 rescuers + 2 journalists); 2 additional men (Benjamin Vizueth, Gustavo Rodriguez-Hernandez) were missing/presumed dead
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 1 day. Total people was 7, not 5. Not classic ESD — downed power line/flood incident.

Notes: Andrew Pasek, 25, an Eagle Scout, was electrocuted while wading through Harvey floodwater to rescue his sister's cat. His ankle hit a submerged live wire (metal plates from prior surgery attracted the current), and he grabbed an energized light pole for support. His last words: 'Don't touch me, I'm dying.' He lay in the water 1.5 hours before power could be cut. The cat (Momo) was eventually rescued. Family sued CenterPoint Energy.
Electrical Source Detail: Submerged live wire contacted ankle (with metal plates from prior surgery); energized yard light pole
Fault Description: Live wire submerged in murky knee-deep floodwater; victim had metal plates in ankle that drew current; grabbed energized light pole for support
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as Aug 11, 2017 (before Hurricane Harvey); correct is Aug 29, 2017
Research Notes: ESDPA date of Aug 11 is before Hurricane Harvey hit. Correct date Aug 29. PETA honored Pasek posthumously.

Notes: On May 14, 2018, a man's grandson was shocked at Lake Taneycomo, Branson, Missouri. The cause was faulty electrical wiring three docks away from the victim's location, demonstrating how far stray voltage can travel through water.
Electrical Source Detail: Man's grandson was shocked; faulty electrical wiring three docks away from victim's location was the source
Fault Description: Faulty electrical wiring three docks away from where victim was shocked
Research Notes: No independent sources found. The 'three docks away' detail is significant — it illustrates the distance stray voltage can travel through water. Lake Taneycomo is primarily a trout fishing lake below Table Rock Dam with relatively few residential docks. The lake name is correctly spelled 'Taneycomo' (portmanteau of Taney County + Missouri).

Notes: On Saturday, June 2, 2018, shortly after 3 p.m., a 17-year-old female from Franklin County was shocked at a dock in the Gills Creek area of Smith Mountain Lake, Virginia. She was holding a boat lift cable with her hand while her foot was in the water, becoming the path of least resistance. She fell into the lake after being shocked and was pulled from the water by another teen. She was transferred to Carilion Roanoke Memorial Hospital with non-life-threatening injuries. The incident prompted Smith Mountain Lake Marine Volunteer Fire Rescue to offer free stray voltage checks; an alarming 96% of nearly 200 docks checked had some stray voltage detected.
Electrical Source Detail: 17-year-old was holding boat lift cable with her hand while foot was in water, becoming path of least resistance; she was shocked, fell into lake, and was pulled out by another teen
Fault Description: Stray voltage from boat lift; aging wiring; metal components of boat lift energized when lowered into water
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date is June 5, 2018 — correct date is June 2, 2018 (Saturday); ESDPA states 'Smith Mountain Lake GA' — Smith Mountain Lake is in VIRGINIA (Bedford/Franklin/Pittsylvania counties), NOT Georgia
Research Notes: ESDPA had TWO major errors: wrong state (GA vs VA) and wrong date (June 5 vs June 2). Smith Mountain Lake is definitively in Virginia. The 96% stray voltage finding from subsequent dock inspections is a significant data point for ESD risk assessment.
All Sources Detail:
WDBJ7 (CBS Roanoke) (news) https://www.wdbj7.com/content/news/Officials-Teen-girl-taken-to-hospital-after-electric-shock-at-Smith-Mountain-Lake-dock-484510931.html [2018-06-04]
Smith Mountain Eagle (news) https://www.smithmountaineagle.com/news/stray-voltage-in-the-water/article_de1fb8dc-6f09-11e8-9d0a-b3f5070c0b25.html [2018-06-13]
SmithMountainLake.com (news) https://smithmountainlake.com/news/local/boat-lifts-a-common-source-of-stray-voltage-in-the-water/article_06cd2b25-a02f-510d-bd38-8046a5393b44.html [2018]

Notes: On Monday, June 11, 2018, swimmers felt electric shock at Camelot Cove, Camdenton, MO on Lake of the Ozarks. A bare energized wire was found dangling in the water from a neighbor's dock. The incident was described as potentially fatal — the wiring problem 'could have killed several people who were swimming in the Lake.' Discovery occurred before anyone was seriously injured.
Electrical Source Detail: Bare energized wire dangling in the water from a neighbor's dock
Fault Description: Bare (uninsulated) energized wire from neighbor's dock dangling into the water
Research Notes: Confirmed by Lake Expo in a dock safety article. Incident referenced in safety warnings rather than standalone news coverage. The 'neighbor's dock' origin highlights how ESD hazards can affect swimmers far from the electrical source. Discovery before serious injury makes this a near-miss.

Facility Name: Private dock near J Road, 5 Mile Marker
Notes: On July 3, 2018, at 2 p.m., a woman was shocked on a metal dock ladder at Rocky Mount near the 5 Mile Marker on Lake of the Ozarks, MO. She reported arcing between the ladder and the rings on her hand while exiting the water. Several small children also reported feeling electrical sensations in the water. Rocky Mount Fire Protection District responded and measured AC voltage levels ranging from 3.5 to 15 volts — levels Fire Chief Kevin Hurtibise identified as 'life threatening.' Investigation revealed a faulty water heater was sending voltage through the ground wire to the dock's electrical system. The fire department required inspection before dock power could be reconnected.
Electrical Source Detail: Faulty water heater was sending voltage through the ground wire to the dock's electrical system; woman reported arcing between dock ladder and rings on her hand
Fault Description: Faulty water heater sending voltage through ground wire to dock electrical system
Research Notes: All ESDPA details confirmed by Lake Expo. Multiple people affected (woman plus several children). Voltage range 3.5–15 VAC confirmed as life-threatening by fire chief. Root cause (faulty water heater via ground wire) is an unusual and instructive mechanism.

Notes: On July 4, 2018, Shelly Colucci felt a tingling sensation in her feet while entering her family's pool in Canfield, Ohio. She exited, tested the water with her hand and felt the same tingle, recognized she was being shocked, and immediately ordered her son out of the pool. Ohio Edison technicians confirmed stray voltage in the water. The family subsequently installed a Shock Alert detection device. Shelly Colucci publicly warned other pool owners to safeguard their pools.
Electrical Source Detail: Stray voltage detected in pool water; Ohio Edison confirmed stray voltage; could be from underground wires or pool equipment
Fault Description: Stray voltage — could be from damaged underground wires or improperly installed pool equipment (wiring, lights, speakers, filters)
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA states 'faulty pool wiring' — source identifies broader 'stray voltage' which could be from underground wires or pool equipment
Research Notes: ESDPA describes cause as 'faulty pool wiring' but source identifies broader 'stray voltage' which could originate from underground utility wires or pool equipment. Quick detection by the mother prevented potential fatality.

Notes: Wesley Seeley, 23, was leaning against a bollard light along Bricktown Canal when it gave way, sending him into the water. He grabbed the bollard to get out and was electrocuted. Brandon Gann jumped in to save him and was also severely shocked. 18 loose bollards were found. Bollards had been scheduled for removal months earlier. Libra Electric found 75% liable.
Legal Outcome: City settled $175K to Seeley estate, $170K to Gann. Jury awarded $8M against Libra Electric (75% liable): $3M to Seeley's mother, $5M to Gann, $500K to Gann's wife.
Electrical Source Detail: Ungrounded, inadequately secured bollard lights along canal
Fault Description: 18 loose bollard lights found; ungrounded; installed by Libra Electric Company
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date wrong by 394 days: listed as Sep 1, 2017; correct is Sep 30, 2018
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by over a year. Bollards had been requested for removal in April 2018.

Facility Name: Dock area at end of Appleton Street
Notes: On November 4, 2018, a woman was walking her 13-year-old black Labrador retriever near Paugus Bay (Lake Winnipesaukee) at the end of Appleton Street in Laconia, NH. The dog escaped and jumped into the water, where it was electrocuted. The owner suffered a powerful electric shock while attempting to rescue the dog and was transported to hospital for evaluation. Laconia Fire Department confirmed electrical current in the water. Eversource shut off power to docks and a light pole and determined the hazard was caused by 'customer-owned equipment.' The dog's body was recovered after power was disconnected.
Electrical Source Detail: Customer-owned electrical fixture (light pole near dock) energized water; dog (13-year-old black Labrador) was electrocuted; owner shocked attempting rescue
Fault Description: Customer-owned privately-installed electrical fixture near dock energized the water; Eversource shut off power to docks and light pole
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date is November 6, 2018 (newspaper publication date); actual incident date was November 4, 2018 (Sunday)
Research Notes: ESDPA date of November 6 was the newspaper publication date; incident occurred Sunday, November 4, 2018. Dog death is the notable element; owner's near-miss while attempting rescue. Eversource (utility) identified cause as customer-owned equipment.
All Sources Detail:
New Hampshire Union Leader (news) https://www.unionleader.com/news/animals/dog-electrocuted-when-it-jumps-into-paugus-bay-owner-suffers-powerful-shock-in-futile-rescue/article_7b67dba8-50fa-526e-a7b9-5208080c5ae1.html [2018-11-06]
CBS Boston (WBZ-TV) (news) https://www.cbsnews.com/boston/news/dog-electrocuted-lake-winnipesaukee-laconia-new-hampshire/ [2018-11-08]
Boston.com (news) https://www.boston.com/news/local-news/2018/11/08/dog-in-new-hampshire-electrocuted-after-jumping-in-water/ [2018-11-08]
NECN (news) https://www.necn.com/news/national-international/dog-electrocuted-after-jumping-in-new-hampshire-lake-winnipesaukee-owner-gets-shocked/2019509/ [2018-11]

Facility Name: Metal pedestrian bridge over Weyland Canal, Dixon Avenue West
Notes: Jacob Hourmouzus (17) and Jacob Schneider (17), both Dixon High School students, were electrocuted after jumping into an irrigation canal to rescue a dog. When they grabbed the metal bridge to get out, they were paralyzed by the current. Schneider's cousin Kelby Holland jumped in and knocked them free. The dog survived. The lawsuit alleged district workers improperly bypassed a circuit breaker.
All Victims: Jacob Hourmouzus (17, M); Jacob Schneider (17, M); Kelby Holland (M)
Legal Outcome: Solano Irrigation District settled for $14 million total ($7M per family) in December 2019.
Electrical Source Detail: District workers improperly bypassed circuit breaker; metal bridge became energized
Fault Description: Solano Irrigation District workers allegedly bypassed a circuit breaker, allowing the metal bridge to become electrified
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date wrong by 6 months AND wrong year: listed Sep 30, 2018; correct is April 1, 2019
Research Notes: ESDPA date wrong by 6 months and the wrong year. $14M settlement reached within 8 months.

Facility Name: Universal Studios Volcano Bay water park
Notes: On June 2, 2019, numerous staff and guests were shocked at Universal Studios Volcano Bay water park in Orlando, FL. Guests reported sensations described as 'zapping,' 'bee stings all over their legs,' and numbness radiating through their bodies. Shocks began shortly after park opening and continued for hours before the park closed early. Five lifeguards were hospitalized and released same day. OSHA investigation found the cause was a construction defect: a grounding rod inadvertently driven through an electrical conduit during construction, damaging wiring and energizing wet surfaces and water throughout the park. OSHA issued no fines, concluding Universal was unaware of the defect. At least one guest (April Carlino) filed a lawsuit.
Legal Outcome: April Carlino (30, Massachusetts) filed lawsuit November 2019 in Orange County Circuit Court for $15,000+. Universal argued no duty to warn because they were unaware of the defect. OSHA found construction errors but issued no fines, concluding Universal was not aware of the defect.
Electrical Source Detail: Construction defect: grounding rod inadvertently driven through electrical conduit during park construction, damaging outer sheath of wiring and energizing wet ground surfaces and water throughout park
Fault Description: During construction, a grounding rod was inadvertently driven through an electrical conduit, damaging wiring. Electrical current was sent through the ground, energizing wet surfaces and water park-wide.
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA states cause was 'water coaster short circuit' — OSHA investigation found it was a construction defect (grounding rod through conduit), not a short circuit in a water coaster; ESDPA states 'No injuries were found after examination' — multiple workers were hospitalized and at least one guest filed a lawsuit claiming injury
Research Notes: ESDPA cause description ('water coaster short circuit') is incorrect per OSHA findings. The problem affected multiple areas park-wide, not a single attraction. ESDPA's 'no injuries' claim is misleading — 5 lifeguards were hospitalized (released same day) and at least one lawsuit was filed. Extensively covered by Orlando media.
All Sources Detail:
Orlando Sentinel (news) https://www.orlandosentinel.com/2019/11/05/universals-volcano-bay-electrical-shock-happened-because-of-construction-errors-osha-finds/ [2019-11-05]
Orlando Sentinel (news) https://www.orlandosentinel.com/2020/01/27/volcano-bay-visitor-sues-universal-in-first-lawsuit-over-electric-shock-incident-universal-blames-her-in-response/ [2020-01-27]
Click Orlando (WKMG) (news) https://www.clickorlando.com/theme-parks/2019/06/06/universal-admits-electrical-issues-at-volcano-bay-sent-workers-to-hospitals/ [2019-06-06]
Inside the Magic (news) https://insidethemagic.net/2019/06/volcano-bay-shocks-started-early/ [2019-06]
Spectrum News 13 (news) https://mynews13.com/fl/orlando/attractions/2020/01/27/volcano-bay-lawsuit-electric-shocks [2020-01-27]

Notes: McKenzie Kinley, 9, was electrocuted while swimming at her father's home in Citrus Heights. She touched a live wire on an unsealed recessed pool light that was under repair. Four other children in the pool were unharmed. The home was built in the late 1950s. Parents believed the light was disconnected from power. Father Cliff Kinley spoke out about pool electrical safety.
Electrical Source Detail: Unsealed pool light fixture under repair; live wire exposed
Fault Description: Recessed pool light fixture was unsealed and under repair; exposed hot wire contacted water; home built in late 1950s with aging electrical
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as April 2; correct is July 14, 2019
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by over 3 months. Some sources say age 10.

Notes: Hunter Morrison, 19, of Fannett TX, was electrocuted while trying to move his horse during Tropical Storm Imelda flooding. First confirmed death from Imelda in Jefferson County. The source of the electricity was never publicly identified. A lightning storm was occurring simultaneously. Jefferson County received 40+ inches of rain in 72 hours.
Electrical Source Detail: Source of electricity not identified; could be downed power line or submerged equipment
Fault Description: Floodwater electrified by unknown source during Tropical Storm Imelda; lightning storm also occurring
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 19; correct is September 19, 2019
Research Notes: ESDPA month wrong (July vs September). Not classic ESD — storm flooding incident.

Facility Name: Private dock (non-electrified; stray voltage from neighbor's dock)
Notes: On May 11, 2020, Meredith Defoe was shocked at her dock on Lewis Smith Lake, Alabama, despite her dock having no electricity installed. Stray voltage was coming from her neighbor's dock. She stated 'It paralyzed me from my feet all the way up.' She attempted to swim toward shore but was shocked more severely when she grabbed a dock cable. Her husband rescued her by extending a pole for her to grab. She stated she would have died without his intervention. Following the incident, she installed a Dock Lifeguard electrical detection device and became an advocate for dock electrical safety.
Electrical Source Detail: Stray voltage from neighbor's dock energized water around victim's non-electrified dock; victim paralyzed from feet upward; husband rescued her with a pole
Fault Description: Stray voltage leaking from neighbor's dock into surrounding water; victim's own dock had no electricity installed
Research Notes: Notable for demonstrating that stray voltage from a neighbor's dock can affect a completely non-electrified dock. Victim became a safety advocate. No contemporaneous news coverage (May 2020 during COVID-19 pandemic); coverage came later through advocacy work in 2021. Lewis Smith Lake spans Cullman, Walker, and Winston counties in Alabama.

Notes: Brothers Timothy Miller (53) and Michael Miller (50) died at Scorpion Bay Marina on Lake Pleasant. Timothy jumped in around 6:30 PM and was immediately overcome. Michael jumped in to rescue him. Timothy's girlfriend also entered and suffered burn marks. Investigation found the victims' own boat had an improper electrical modification that bypassed safety features, discharging electricity around the swim platform. Marina systems were not at fault.
All Victims: Timothy Miller (53, M); Michael Miller (50, M); Unknown (F)
Electrical Source Detail: Boat had incompatible electrical connection system; safety features intentionally bypassed via improper modification
Fault Description: Victims' boat had incompatible shore power connector; electrical safety features bypassed by improper modification discharged electricity around swim platform
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date wrong: listed Sep 19, 2019; correct is July 12, 2020 (wrong month AND wrong year)
Research Notes: ESDPA date wrong by ~10 months and wrong year. Cause was the victims' own boat modification, not the marina.

Notes: Khaleel Marcos Reynolds, 15, was electrocuted in the North Villa Inn motel pool in Houston. Someone had removed an interior pool light, leaving wires exposed. The pool had failed inspections in July and December 2018 and was never permitted to operate. The family lived at the motel where his mother worked; she was reportedly fired and evicted after the death. Both parents filed wrongful death lawsuits.
Electrical Source Detail: Interior pool light removed from housing, leaving wires exposed
Fault Description: Someone removed interior pool light from housing, leaving wires exposed; pool had failed inspections in 2018 and was never permitted to operate
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 12; correct is August 29, 2020
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by ~7 weeks. Pool was operating illegally — never had a permit. LOA1-C news archive and LOA11 YouTube research found KPRC 2/Click2Houston videos covering the incident and family vigil. Pool had failed Harris County inspections in July and December 2018.

Notes: Mark Mixon, 65, was electrocuted while sandbagging his Bradenton Beach home during Tropical Storm Eta. He stepped into the flooded laundry room containing approximately 4 inches of water and a plugged-in dryer. A firefighter was also injured attempting to reach the scene. EMS had to wait for FPL to cut power. Not classic dock/marina ESD — storm flooding/indoor electrocution.
Electrical Source Detail: Plugged-in clothes dryer in 4 inches of Tropical Storm Eta flood water
Fault Description: Dryer plugged in during Tropical Storm Eta flooding; 4 inches of standing water in laundry room
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date Aug 30 is meteorologically impossible — TS Eta hit Florida in November 2020
Research Notes: ESDPA date meteorologically impossible — Tropical Storm Eta was in November 2020, not August.
All Sources Detail:
Tampa Bay Times (news) https://www.tampabay.com/hurricane/2020/11/12/beach-resident-electrocuted-putting-out-sandbags-for-tropical-storm-eta-officials-say/ [2020-11-12]
FOX 13 Tampa Bay (news) https://www.fox13news.com/news/manatee-county-prepares-for-eta [2020-11-11]

Notes: Trevor Cate, 18, a 2021 Clyde High School graduate, was electrocuted at Clyde Lake while swimming and towing a disabled sailboat. The sailboat struck a power line, which broke and fell into the water. Lake officials had to cut power before rescuers could reach him. He was transported to Hendrick Medical Center and died June 3.
Electrical Source Detail: Sailboat struck overhead power line while being towed; broken line fell into water
Fault Description: Disabled sailboat being swim-towed struck overhead power line; line broke and contacted water, electrifying area
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date wrong: Nov 4, 2020; correct is June 2-3, 2021 (wrong month, day, AND year)
Research Notes: ESDPA date wrong on month, day, and year. Incident just days after his high school graduation.

Facility Name: 3700 block of North Palm Leaf Circle, La Homa
Notes: Gustavo Delgado, 14, was electrocuted while walking through a flooded street in the La Homa area near Mission, TX. He stepped into a puddle containing a submerged extension cord that ran from a house to a trailer home. He was reportedly attempting to unplug the cord. Severe thunderstorms had caused flooding in the area. Not classic dock/marina ESD — extension cord in floodwater.
Electrical Source Detail: Extension cord connecting house to trailer home, fully submerged in floodwater
Fault Description: Extension cord used as permanent connection between buildings, submerged in storm floodwater; no GFCI protection
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date off by 1 day: listed June 3; correct is June 4, 2021
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 1 day. June 3 was the storm date; June 4 was the electrocution date.

Notes: James DeAngelo, 23, of Venetia PA, was found unresponsive while swimming near a docked boat at Beach Club Marina in New Eagle on the Monongahela River around 7:30 PM. A friend who jumped in to search 'felt horribly shocked.' Three others in the area also suffered shocks, strongly supporting ESD diagnosis. Initial 911 call reported electrical current in the water.
Electrical Source Detail: Electricity in water near docked boat; source not publicly determined
Fault Description: Stray electrical current in water near marina; 3 others also shocked
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as June 4; correct is July 4, 2021 (exactly 1 month off)
Research Notes: ESDPA date exactly 1 month early. July 4 was a Sunday, matching reports; June 4 was a Friday. LOA11 YouTube research found WTAE-TV Pittsburgh videos covering investigation and 2023 hockey tribute. LOA6 community research independently confirmed. James DeAngelo Foundation formed for ESD awareness; hockey community tribute held October 2023. DeAngelo played for California University of Pennsylvania Vulcans Hockey Club.

Facility Name: Prospect Yacht Club / Drift Bar, 1900 Victory Lane
Notes: William Keith Elkins, 58, a self-employed commercial diver, was electrocuted while working underwater at Prospect Yacht Club preparing barges for Thunder Over Louisville. Kentucky issued a stop work order 2 days before his death due to lack of building permits. State inspectors later found 33 electrical violations with electricity leaking at 4+ amps. The fire marshal shut down the yacht club indefinitely. OSHA declined to investigate (independent contractor). Yacht club's attorney disputed the electrocution finding.
Electrical Source Detail: 33 electrical violations documented; electricity leaking from equipment (4+ amps in one location)
Fault Description: Yacht club had 33 electrical violations; stop work order issued 2 days before death; electricity leaking from equipment at dangerous levels
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date wrong by ~10 months: listed July 4, 2021; correct is April 22, 2022
Research Notes: ESDPA date wrong by ~10 months. OSHA jurisdictional gap — no agency investigated despite 33 violations.

Facility Name: Marina (location not published in OSHA summary)
Notes: At 3:45 PM on August 3, 2022, a marina employee operating a mobile boat lift to move boats into slips was electrocuted when the lift contacted overhead power lines. He fell from the operator's cab and was pronounced dead at the scene. NAICS 713930 (Marinas). The victim was not in or near water — this is an overhead power line contact, not ESD by mechanism. However, it documents OSHA investigation of a marina-setting fatality.
Electrical Source Detail: Mobile boat lift (marina crane) contacted overhead high-power lines
Fault Description: Mobile boat lift boom contacted overhead power lines while operator was moving boats into slips; operator electrocuted and fell from cab
Research Notes: NOT ESD by mechanism — overhead power line strike while in operator cab on land. Marina setting but no water involvement. Significant for confirming OSHA investigates marina fatalities under NAICS 713930. OSHA Accident Summary Nr 148483.015.

Facility Name: Russell Woods Apartments, 11500 block of LaSalle Street
Notes: PROBABLE PHANTOM DUPLICATE. This entry (FI-2022-001, dated Aug 16, 2022) has word-for-word identical description to FI-2023-001 (dated Feb 7, 2023). All evidence points to a single incident: the death of James Stanley, 62, on February 7, 2023, at Russell Woods Apartments in Detroit. No news coverage exists for any similar Detroit basement electrocution in August 2022. See ESD-2023-02-07-1 for the actual incident record.
Electrical Source Detail: Live wire in 3-4 feet of basement flood water; entire building was electrically charged
Fault Description: Live wire in flooded apartment building basement; DTE Energy had to cut power; entire building was charged
ESDPA Data Issues: PROBABLE PHANTOM DUPLICATE of FI-2023-001; ESDPA date Aug 16, 2022 has no supporting evidence — identical description to Feb 7, 2023 incident; No news coverage exists for a basement electrocution in Detroit in Aug 2022
Research Notes: This file exists as a cross-reference for the ESDPA phantom duplicate. The real incident is Feb 7, 2023 (James Stanley). The Aug 2022 date appears to be a data entry error in the ESDPA document.

Facility Name: Russell Woods Apartments, 11500 block of LaSalle Street at Webb
Notes: James Stanley, 62, of Oakland County (Pontiac area), was electrocuted at 6:17 PM while snaking a drain in the flooded basement of Russell Woods Apartments on Detroit's west side. He stepped in standing water containing a live wire. The entire building was electrically charged through 3-4 feet of flood water. Fire crews had to wait for DTE Energy to shut off power. His wife Sherri: 'the 29 years we were married were the best years.' Not classic dock/marina ESD — indoor flooded basement.
Electrical Source Detail: Live wire in 3-4 feet of basement flood water; entire building was electrically charged
Fault Description: Plumber (retired from Doetsch Environmental Services) was snaking a drain; basement had 3-4 ft of water with live wire; entire building became charged; DTE Energy required to cut power
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA says 'flooded home basement'; actually an apartment complex (Russell Woods Apartments); FI-2022-001 appears to be a phantom duplicate of this entry
Research Notes: ESDPA date correct (Feb 7, 2023). FI-2022-001 is a phantom duplicate with wrong date. Building managed by VEOT Group (Florida-based).

Facility Name: Wright Island Marina (Drake Ave. Marina, Inc.)
Notes: Dr. Kole Shtjefni Nikprelaj, 24, of Bedford NY, and his girlfriend Dila Bujaj had just finished jet-skiing at Wright Island Marina in New Rochelle when both were electrocuted by a live wire in the water near the dock/boat ramp. Both were found unconscious in the water by a bystander and pulled out. Bujaj survived; Nikprelaj was placed on life support and died July 30, 2023, approximately three weeks after the incident. Fire officials confirmed the boat ramp had become electrified.
Legal Outcome: Wrongful death lawsuit filed Nov 11, 2024: Lisa Nikpreljaj v. ELQ Industries, Inc., Verde Electric Corp., M F Electrical Service Co., Inc., and Drake Ave. Marina, Inc. d/b/a Wright Island Marina (Westchester County Supreme Court).
Electrical Source Detail: Live wire electrified boat ramp at marina
Fault Description: Boat ramp became electrified by live wire; fire officials confirmed electrification of ramp
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA website news section references this incident (July 9, 2023: '2 shocked after boat ramp at New Rochelle marina becomes electrified') but it is NOT in the ESDPA incident list
Research Notes: Not in ESDPA incident list, though ESDPA website news section acknowledges the incident. Found via LOA1-E news archive research. Wrongful death lawsuit names multiple electrical contractors (ELQ Industries, Verde Electric, MF Electrical Service). Nikprelaj was a doctor; remembered at wake as 'wake up and be thankful for every day.'

Facility Name: Private dock, Mid Lake Heights Drive
Notes: Dr. Marshall L. Meadors III, 65, of Anderson SC, was found dead in the water at Lake Hartwell near Mid Lake Heights Drive. He went missing Monday evening; authorities were called around 8 PM. Investigators found exposed electrical wires on an electrical box providing power to the dock. The Anderson County Coroner ruled the preliminary cause of death as freshwater drowning possibly secondary to electrocution. An autopsy was scheduled.
Electrical Source Detail: Exposed electrical wires on electrical box providing power to dock
Fault Description: Exposed electrical wires found on electrical box providing power to the dock
Research Notes: Not in ESDPA list. Found via LOA1-E news archive research. Lake Hartwell is a large Army Corps of Engineers reservoir on the SC-GA border. Coroner's preliminary ruling suggests electrocution but awaiting final determination. Three independent news sources confirm the incident details.

Facility Name: Private dock near Dove Trail, Lanier Beach South Road area
Notes: Thomas 'Shep' Milner, 24, jumped off his family's dock into Lake Lanier near Dove Trail in Forsyth County on July 27, 2023. He was heard screaming for help shortly after entering the water. A neighbor jumped in to rescue him but immediately felt a burning sensation he recognized as electrical shock. The neighbor swam ashore, turned off the dock power box, then re-entered to pull Milner out. Milner's uncle administered CPR until first responders arrived. Milner was transported to Northside Forsyth Hospital and died July 28. Georgia DNR confirmed electricity came from a nearby power box. His mother Martha Milner said the dock was less than 3 years old and outfitted by a licensed electrician.
Electrical Source Detail: Electricity from nearby power box supplying dock boat lift energized the water
Fault Description: Dock less than 3 years old, outfitted with electricity by licensed electrician; power box supplying boat lift energized lake water near dock
Research Notes: Not in ESDPA list. Found via LOA1-E news archive research, independently confirmed by LOA4 government records (Forsyth County Sheriff investigation) and LOA11 YouTube research (5 videos from major Atlanta TV stations). Lake Lanier has a prior documented ESD near-miss: NM-2013-003 (May 27, 2013). Cardinal News (Aug 7, 2023) cited this death alongside ESDPA statistics.

Facility Name: Harbourside Place outdoor shopping center fountain, 149 Soundings Ave
Notes: Nathaniel 'Nate' Davenport, 45, a Navy veteran and father of four, was electrocuted at the Harbourside Place fountain in Jupiter, FL on October 22, 2023 at approximately 3:33 PM. Three of his children entered the interactive fountain/splash pad; two boys appeared face-down and distressed. Davenport jumped in to rescue them and immediately collapsed from electrocution. Bystanders located an emergency power shutoff. Eight people total required medical attention including three Davenport children and at least one other adult. Davenport was transported to Jupiter Medical Center and pronounced dead approximately one hour later. A prior incident on July 10, 2023, had shocked 15+ people at the same fountain (witness photographed visible wire); a Harbourside employee dismissed the concern and the fountain remained operational.
All Victims: Nathaniel Davenport (45, M); Unknown Davenport (M); Unknown Davenport (M); Unknown Davenport (M)
Legal Outcome: Wrongful death lawsuit filed Dec 27, 2023. Out-of-court settlement reached during mediation March 6, 2024 (terms undisclosed). Carnegie Hero Fund posthumous recognition.
Electrical Source Detail: Light fixtures within fountain were leaking electrical voltage due to ground fault or improper system
Fault Description: Fountain light fixtures leaking voltage; Town of Jupiter Building Dept declared fountain electrical system unsafe; no GFCI protection apparent; fountain builder Van Kirk & Sons had not performed work in 5-7 years
Research Notes: Not in ESDPA list — fountain electrocution outside ESDPA's marina/dock scope. Found via LOA3 legal research and LOA4 government records (Jupiter Police 149-page incident report, Town of Jupiter Building Dept 74-page unsafe declaration). Prior July 2023 incident with 15+ shocked represents clear regulatory failure. James DeAngelo Foundation (separate PA incident) recognized Davenport's heroism.

Facility Name: Private dock, Lee Drive, Craddock Creek area
Notes: Jesse Cyrus Hamric, 18, from Clark/Steamboat Springs CO, was electrocuted at 3:44 AM on July 4, 2024, at a private dock on Smith Mountain Lake. A submerged boat lift (20+ years old) was producing 116+ volts. Two friends who jumped in to help were also shocked. First documented ESD death at Smith Mountain Lake. Retired firefighter Neil Harrington had tested 200+ SML docks and found stray voltage at nearly all. New dock electrical codes adopted by three counties. The Jesse Cyrus Hamric Foundation for Courage and Love was established. GoFundMe raised $111K+.
All Victims: Jesse Cyrus Hamric (18, M); Unknown; Unknown
Electrical Source Detail: Submerged electric boat lift producing 116+ volts; lift at least 20 years old; dock not recently inspected
Fault Description: Stray voltage from submerged boat lift; voltage readings 'off the charts' per fire dept; readings normalized when lift raised from water
Research Notes: ESDPA date correct. Received BBC, USA Today, and international coverage. 26 independent sources found — most extensively documented incident in dataset.

Notes: A 13-year-old boy from Fulton County GA (Atlanta metro) was electrocuted trying to exit the water via a metal dock ladder at Blue Ridge Lake. Two boys attempting rescue and a woman were also shocked. Fannin County Coroner Becky Callihan is investigating. The victim was a certified scuba diver, making accidental drowning unlikely. Numerous electrical deficiencies found at the dock. Limited news coverage; primary local article is paywalled.
Electrical Source Detail: Numerous electrical deficiencies found at private dock
Fault Description: Numerous electrical deficiencies found by Fannin County official; specific details not yet public
Research Notes: ESDPA date correct. Very limited independent coverage — only local Blue Ridge newspaper (paywalled). Victim name partially visible as 'Ha...' in truncated coroner quote. LOA4 government records research confirmed Fannin County investigation found numerous electrical deficiencies at dock. Victim was from Fulton County (Atlanta metro), confirmed as certified scuba diver.

Facility Name: Marina Shores at Dune Harbor, D dock
Notes: Gabriel Gonzalez, 21, of Hebron IN, was electrocuted at Marina Shores on Burns Waterway. He dove in to retrieve a cooler that fell from a boat (slip holder told him to let it go). He felt a 'tingling sensation' before going under. His brother Francis (23) jumped in and was also overcome; a bystander threw a flotation device. Gabriel's body was recovered ~39 minutes later. Autopsy: drowning secondary to electrocution. A similar incident had occurred at the same marina just 4 days earlier. Marina lacked required NEC warning signs.
All Victims: Gabriel Gonzalez (21, M); Francis Gonzalez III (23, M)
Legal Outcome: Schafer & Schafer LLP filed wrongful death complaint seeking $50-100M against Marina Shores entities. Prior electrical shock event occurred at same marina 4 days earlier.
Electrical Source Detail: Equipment from nearby slip holder; prior incident 4 days earlier from 30-amp plug in 50-amp outlet
Fault Description: Electricity from equipment of nearby slip holder; marina lacked mandatory NEC Article 555.24 warning signs
ESDPA Data Issues: ESDPA date listed as July 11; correct is July 10, 2025 (Thursday). July 11 was the publication date.
Research Notes: ESDPA date off by 1 day (publication date vs incident date). Prior incident at same marina is highly significant.
